function.en: Collected taxes, prepared budgets, and managed state trade receipts and fiscal accounting

function.zh: 监督牧业统计、合作化试验、兽医服务和饲料储备
function.en: Oversaw pastoral statistics, cooperative experiments, veterinary services, and fodder reserves

function.zh: 负责学校、扫盲、宣传、出版和反宗教文化改造
function.en: Supervised schools, literacy campaigns, propaganda, publishing, and anti-religious cultural transformation

function.zh: 管理法院、检察、监狱和政治案件的法律程序
function.en: Managed courts, procuracy, prisons, and legal procedures including political cases

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为多个省级“艾马克”和下属“苏木”，地方政府由党组织、行政干部与安全人员共同控制，牧区治理高度依赖基层干部与流动行政。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provincial aimags and subordinate sums; local government was jointly controlled by party organizations, administrative cadres, and security personnel, with pastoral administration relying heavily on local cadres and mobile governance.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系名义上依据人民共和国宪制与革命法令，实际上兼具党令、行政命令和政治审判特征；在大清洗时期，安全机构对司法有压倒性影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system nominally rested on republican constitutional law and revolutionary decrees, but in practice it combined party directives, administrative orders, and political trials; during the Great Purge, the security organs exercised overwhelming influence over justice.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自畜牧税、营业税、国家贸易利润、关税及国营与合作机构上缴；直接税规模有限，实物征收在牧区仍很重要。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from livestock taxes, turnover taxes, state trade profits, customs duties, and remittances from state and cooperative bodies; direct taxation remained limited, and collection in kind was still important in pastoral areas.

religion.zh: 传统上以藏传佛教格鲁派为主，但到1939年宗教机构遭到严重镇压，大量寺院被关闭，国家奉行官方无神论。
religion.en: Traditionally dominated by Tibetan Buddhism of the Gelug school, but by 1939 religious institutions had been severely suppressed, many monasteries closed, and the state promoted official atheism.

philosophy.zh: 官方意识形态为马克思列宁主义，辅以蒙古民族革命叙事、反封建与反宗教宣传，并强调与苏联的革命友谊。
philosophy.en: The official ideology was Marxism-Leninism, combined with a Mongolian nationalist revolutionary narrative, anti-feudal and anti-religious propaganda, and emphasis on revolutionary friendship with the Soviet Union.

writingSystem.zh: 1939年仍主要使用传统蒙古文，国家也开始扩大西里尔化和苏式教育影响，为1940年代文字改革作准备。
writingSystem.en: In 1939 the traditional Mongolian script was still chiefly used, while the state was also expanding Soviet-style education and moves toward Cyrillicization in preparation for the script reforms of the 1940s.

culturalAchievements.zh: 国家建立现代学校、印刷出版体系、革命戏剧与宣传艺术；同时对传统佛教艺术和寺院文化造成巨大破坏。民族音乐、长调与口传史诗仍在民间延续。
culturalAchievements.en: The state built modern schools, print publishing, revolutionary theater, and propaganda art; at the same time it inflicted immense destruction on traditional Buddhist art and monastic culture. Folk music, long song traditions, and oral epics continued among the people.

languageFamily.zh: 蒙古语属于蒙古语族，喀尔喀蒙古语为国家主导方言；俄语在军政与技术领域影响上升。
languageFamily.en: Mongolian belongs to the Mongolic language family, with Khalkha Mongolian as the dominant state dialect; Russian influence was rising in military, governmental, and technical fields.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模较小，以畜牧业为绝对主体，工业化处于早期阶段，国家贸易和苏联援助对财政与物资供应至关重要。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was small and overwhelmingly pastoral, with industrialization at an early stage; state trade and Soviet assistance were crucial for fiscal stability and material supply.

mainIndustries.zh: 游牧畜牧业、羊毛与皮革加工、肉类与畜产品收购、运输、基础建筑、少量采矿和轻工业
mainIndustries.en: Nomadic pastoralism, wool and leather processing, meat and livestock procurement, transport, basic construction, limited mining, and light industry

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以羊毛、皮张、牲畜、肉类和毛皮为主；进口以机械、武器、燃料、布匹、日用品和工业制成品为主，贸易对象主要为苏联。
tradeGoods.en: Exports consisted mainly of wool, hides, livestock, meat, and furs; imports included machinery, arms, fuel, textiles, consumer goods, and manufactured products, with the Soviet Union as the principal trading partner.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以牲畜数量体现，城市居民则拥有有限现金与耐用品；国家对私有牧群与贸易活动的控制不断加强。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was chiefly measured in livestock holdings, while urban residents possessed limited cash and durable goods; state control over private herds and commerce was steadily increasing.

economicSystem.zh: 国家主导的社会主义过渡经济，结合牧区传统家庭生产、合作化尝试、国营贸易和苏联导向的计划配置
economicSystem.en: A state-led transitional socialist economy combining traditional pastoral household production, cooperative experiments, state trading, and Soviet-directed planned allocation

source.zh: 国营企业与合作机构上缴
source.en: Remittances from state enterprises and cooperatives

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模有限，现金储备和外汇能力薄弱，财政平衡高度依赖国家贸易和苏联信贷、补助及军事支持。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was limited, with weak cash reserves and foreign exchange capacity; fiscal balance depended heavily on state trade and on Soviet credits, subsidies, and military support.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策优先保障边防、军费、安全机构和国家建设，同时通过国营贸易和征收畜牧税从牧区汲取资源，赤字可由苏联支持弥补。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized border defense, military spending, security organs, and state-building, while extracting resources from the pastoral economy through state trade and livestock taxation; deficits could be covered through Soviet support.

name.en: Mongolian People's Revolutionary Army Ground Forces

description.zh: 以骑兵、摩托化支援单位、步兵和边防部队为主，是保卫边境和配合苏军作战的核心力量。
description.en: Composed mainly of cavalry, motorized support units, infantry, and border forces, it was the core force for frontier defense and for operations alongside the Red Army.

description.zh: 规模很小，依赖苏联训练、顾问和装备，主要承担侦察、联络和有限支援任务。
description.en: Very small in scale and dependent on Soviet training, advisors, and equipment, it performed reconnaissance, liaison, and limited support missions.

name.zh: 内务部边防与治安部队
name.en: Internal Affairs Border and Security Troops

description.zh: 负责边境巡逻、反渗透、交通线保卫和国内政治控制，在对日边境压力下作用突出。
description.en: Responsible for border patrol, anti-infiltration, line-of-communication security, and domestic political control, these forces were especially important under pressure along the Japanese frontier.

commanderInChief.zh: 霍尔洛·乔巴山
commanderInChief.en: Khorloogiin Choibalsan

technology.zh: 军队处于1930年代末水平，仍保留强烈骑兵传统，但在苏联援助下拥有机枪、火炮、装甲车辆、无线电和少量军机；重装备和后勤高度依赖苏联。
technology.en: The military stood at a late-1930s level, retaining a strong cavalry tradition but, with Soviet aid, fielding machine guns, artillery, armored vehicles, radios, and a small number of aircraft; heavy equipment and logistics were highly dependent on the Soviet Union.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自日本帝国及其控制下的满洲国边境军力，1939年哈拉哈河—诺门罕地区冲突表明战争风险真实存在；内部则警惕所谓反革命、泛蒙古主义和边境渗透。
threats.en: The principal threat came from the Japanese Empire and the forces of Manchukuo along the frontier; the 1939 clashes in the Khalkhin Gol-Nomonhan area demonstrated that the danger of war was real. Internally, the regime remained alert to alleged counterrevolutionaries, pan-Mongol movements, and border infiltration.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 喷火/零式/Bf109战斗机，争夺制空权
description.en: Spitfire/Zero/Bf109 fighter for air superiority

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

description.zh: 舰载机母舰，太平洋战争主角
description.en: Carrier launching aircraft, star of the Pacific War

populationDescription.zh: 人口稀少且分散，绝大多数为喀尔喀蒙古人及其他蒙古族群，城市人口有限，乌兰巴托为唯一真正意义上的全国性政治中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was sparse and dispersed, consisting overwhelmingly of Khalkha Mongols and other Mongol groups, with a limited urban population; Ulaanbaatar was the only true nationwide political center.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括游牧牧民、国家干部、军人、城市工人、小商贩与运输人员；旧王公贵族和喇嘛集团在政治与经济上已被系统性打击和边缘化。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata included nomadic herders, state cadres, soldiers, urban workers, small traders, and transport personnel; the old aristocracy and lama establishment had been systematically attacked and marginalized politically and economically.

allies.zh: 苏联是唯一决定性盟友，并在军事、经济和外交上提供保护；与共产国际体系保持一致。
allies.en: The Soviet Union was the only decisive ally, providing military, economic, and diplomatic protection; Mongolia aligned with the Comintern sphere.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象为日本帝国及其扶植的满洲国；对日本在中国北方和内蒙古的扩张高度戒备。
enemies.en: The main hostile powers were the Japanese Empire and its client state Manchukuo; the republic was highly wary of Japanese expansion in North China and Inner Mongolia.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以生存和边境安全为核心，几乎完全依附苏联，通过紧密军事合作维护独立，同时在国际上反对日本扩张并争取被视为主权国家。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on survival and frontier security and was almost entirely tied to the Soviet Union, using close military cooperation to preserve independence while opposing Japanese expansion and seeking recognition as a sovereign state.

era.zh: 第二次世界大战时代

keyInnovations.zh: 主要现代化成果包括苏式军队训练、无线电通信、机动车运输、基础航空能力、国家印刷与宣传体系、现代学校教育和初步公共卫生网络。
keyInnovations.en: Key modernization achievements included Soviet-style military training, radio communications, motor transport, basic aviation capability, a state printing and propaganda system, modern schooling, and a rudimentary public health network.

strengths.zh: 政权在1939年总体稳定，拥有明确的党国控制体系，并得到苏联强力支持；骑兵传统适合草原作战，首都与核心行政区控制较强。
strengths.en: The regime was broadly stable in 1939, possessed a clear party-state control system, and enjoyed strong Soviet backing; its cavalry tradition suited steppe warfare, and control over the capital and core administrative zones was strong.

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础薄弱，工业能力有限，对苏联依赖极深；大清洗破坏了宗教、社会和行政结构，也削弱了人才储备与社会信任。
weaknesses.en: Its economic base was weak, industrial capacity limited, and dependence on the Soviet Union profound; the Great Purge had damaged religious, social, and administrative structures and also depleted skilled personnel and public trust.

outlook.zh: 短期内只要苏联继续支持，蒙古人民共和国能够维持独立并抵御边境压力；但其未来仍取决于苏日力量对比以及战争是否扩大到东北亚草原地带。
outlook.en: In the short term, so long as Soviet support continued, the Mongolian People's Republic could preserve its independence and withstand border pressure; its future nonetheless depended on the balance between Soviet and Japanese power and on whether war widened across the Northeast Asian steppe.

description.zh: 蒙古人民共和国在苏联影响下保持独立，并与日本势力在边境对峙。
description.en: The Mongolian People's Republic remained independent under Soviet influence and faced Japanese forces on its borders.

governmentForm.zh: 日本帝国中央集权下的总督府殖民统治
governmentForm.en: Centralized Governor-General colonial administration under the Empire of Japan

socialStructure.zh: 社会呈殖民等级结构，日本人居于政治、经济与法律上的上层，朝鲜人构成绝大多数劳动人口与地方社会主体；城市中有受教育的朝鲜中产、商人和官吏阶层，但整体上受殖民警察国家严密控制。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized in a colonial hierarchy in which Japanese settlers and officials occupied the upper political, economic, and legal positions, while Koreans made up the overwhelming majority of laborers and local communities. In the cities there existed an educated Korean middle stratum of merchants, professionals, and minor officials, but overall society was tightly controlled by a colonial police state.

rulingClass.zh: 朝鲜总督府高官、日本驻朝军警、日本移民地主与大企业经营者，以及与殖民政权合作的部分朝鲜地主和实业精英
rulingClass.en: Senior officials of the Governor-General's administration, Japanese military and police personnel in Korea, Japanese settler landlords and major corporate managers, together with a limited group of Korean landlords and business elites collaborating with colonial rule

succession.zh: 总督由日本天皇名义任命、实际由东京政府决定更替，并非本地世袭或选举产生
succession.en: The Governor-General was appointed in the Emperor's name and effectively selected by the government in Tokyo; succession was neither hereditary nor locally elective

structure.zh: 1939年的朝鲜由朝鲜总督府直接统治，总督集行政、警察、立法命令与相当广泛的司法监督权于一身，下辖总务、内务、财务、殖产、学务、警务、铁道、专卖等机关，并通过道、府、郡、面、邑、警察网和宪兵体系贯彻统治。
structure.en: In 1939 Korea was ruled directly by the Government-General, whose governor-general concentrated executive authority, police command, ordinance-making power, and broad judicial oversight. Beneath him stood departments for general affairs, internal administration, finance, industry and agriculture, education, police, railways, monopolies, and related bureaus, with control implemented through provinces, urban municipalities, counties, townships, and an extensive police and gendarmerie network.

function.zh: 负责总督府文书、干部任免、行政协调、机密事务与整体政策执行监督
function.en: Handled official documentation, personnel appointments, interdepartmental coordination, confidential matters, and supervision of overall policy execution

function.zh: 负责地方行政、户籍、卫生、土木、社会管制与地方动员
function.en: Oversaw local administration, household registration, public health, civil engineering, social control, and local mobilization

function.zh: 负责预算、税收、国有财产、关税协调与财政监督
function.en: Managed budgets, taxation, state property, tariff coordination, and fiscal supervision

function.zh: 主管农业改良、林业、矿业、工业开发、渔业与资源统制
function.en: Directed agricultural improvement, forestry, mining, industrial development, fisheries, and resource control

function.zh: 负责学校体系、教材审查、皇民化教育、教师管理与文化宣传
function.en: Administered the school system, textbook censorship, imperial-subject indoctrination, teacher management, and cultural propaganda

function.zh: 负责治安、思想监控、反殖民镇压、审查、集会管制与特别高等警察事务
function.en: Controlled public order, ideological surveillance, anti-colonial repression, censorship, assembly regulation, and special higher police matters

function.zh: 经营朝鲜主要铁路干线、运输调度、战时后勤与港口衔接
function.en: Operated major Korean rail trunk lines, traffic scheduling, wartime logistics, and connections to ports

function.zh: 管理烟草、人参及若干专卖项目，为殖民财政提供稳定收入
function.en: Administered tobacco, ginseng, and selected state monopoly items, providing stable revenue to the colonial treasury

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政按道、府、郡、岛、邑、面分层组织。1930年代末的制度虽在形式上扩大了地方机构，但实权仍牢牢掌握在总督府及其任命官僚手中；警察驻在所深入乡村，是最直接的基层控制机关。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by provinces, urban municipalities, counties, islands, towns, and townships. Although local institutions were formally expanded in the late 1930s, real authority remained firmly in the hands of the Government-General and its appointed bureaucrats; police substations in villages were the most immediate organs of grassroots control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以日本帝国法令、朝鲜总督府令与特别警察规制为基础，名义上具有近代法院制度，但政治案件、思想案件与治安案件常受行政权与警察权深刻支配。殖民统治下存在对朝鲜人的差别待遇与广泛的预防性镇压。
legalSystem.en: The legal system rested on imperial Japanese law, ordinances of the Governor-General, and special police regulations. It possessed a formal modern court structure, but political, ideological, and public-order cases were heavily shaped by administrative and police power. Colonial rule involved discriminatory treatment of Koreans and extensive preventive repression.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自土地税、营业税、消费税、专卖收入、铁路收益及各类规费；在战时统制加强后，税制与价格管制、配给和强制储蓄政策相互配合，以保障帝国动员。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from land taxes, business taxes, consumption taxes, monopoly receipts, railway earnings, and various fees. As wartime controls intensified, taxation was increasingly coordinated with price controls, rationing, and compulsory savings measures to support imperial mobilization.

religion.zh: 社会宗教结构复杂，包括儒家祭祀传统、佛教、基督教与民间信仰；殖民当局积极推行神社参拜和皇国臣民化，将其作为政治忠诚教育的一部分。
religion.en: The religious landscape was mixed, including Confucian ancestral rites, Buddhism, Christianity, and folk beliefs; the colonial authorities actively promoted Shinto shrine worship and imperial subjectification as part of political loyalty training.

philosophy.zh: 传统思想基础仍以儒家伦理、家族秩序和教育重视为核心，同时城市知识界受到民族主义、社会主义、自由主义及日本国家主义压力的共同影响。
philosophy.en: Traditional thought remained grounded in Confucian ethics, family hierarchy, and reverence for education, while urban intellectual circles were influenced by nationalism, socialism, liberalism, and the pressures of Japanese statism.

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系同时使用朝鲜谚文、汉字与日文；在1939年前后，殖民政府强化日语教育与公文日文化，逐步压缩韩文公共空间。
writingSystem.en: The writing system involved Korean hangul, Chinese characters, and Japanese; around 1939 the colonial government intensified Japanese-language education and the Japanization of official documents, gradually shrinking public space for Korean writing.

culturalAchievements.zh: 尽管受到审查和同化压力，朝鲜在文学、新闻、音乐、电影、现代绘画和宗教教育领域仍有持续发展。殖民时期也推动了铁路、城市规划、工业景观和近代学校体系的扩张，但这些建设主要服务于帝国统治与资源汲取。
culturalAchievements.en: Despite censorship and assimilation pressures, Korea continued to produce literature, journalism, music, film, modern painting, and religious education. The colonial period also expanded railways, urban planning, industrial landscapes, and modern schooling, though these developments primarily served imperial control and resource extraction.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言为朝鲜语，通常归为朝鲜语系；官方强制推广日语，形成明显的殖民双语不平等格局。
languageFamily.en: The principal language was Korean, usually classified within the Koreanic family; Japanese was officially enforced, producing a strongly unequal colonial bilingual order.

gdpDescription.zh: 1939年的朝鲜经济已高度纳入日本帝国体系，农业仍吸纳多数人口，但北部重工业、化学工业与水电开发增长显著。经济总量在殖民地中相当可观，然而收益分配极不平等，大量利润、粮食与原料被输往日本本土与帝国军需部门。
gdpDescription.en: By 1939 Korea's economy was deeply integrated into the Japanese imperial system. Agriculture still employed the majority of the population, but heavy industry, chemicals, and hydroelectric development in the north had grown markedly. Aggregate output was substantial for a colony, yet distribution was highly unequal, and large shares of profits, grain, and raw materials were transferred to Japan proper and to imperial war needs.

mainIndustries.zh: 稻米与杂粮农业、渔业、矿业（煤、铁、金、钨等）、化学工业、化肥、水电、纺织、铁路运输、港口装卸与轻工业加工
mainIndustries.en: Rice and mixed-grain agriculture, fisheries, mining (including coal, iron, gold, and tungsten), chemicals, fertilizer production, hydroelectric power, textiles, rail transport, port handling, and light industrial processing

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与调拨品主要有稻米、水产、矿石、金属、化肥、化工产品、人参与工业原料；输入品包括日本制成品、机械、军需物资、棉纱与资本设备。
tradeGoods.en: Exports and imperial transfers chiefly included rice, marine products, ores, metals, fertilizer, chemical products, ginseng, and industrial raw materials; imports included Japanese manufactures, machinery, military supplies, cotton yarn, and capital equipment.

name.en: Bank of Chosen notes / yen system

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大。日本移民、公司雇员和部分大地主拥有较高资产，而多数朝鲜农户负债沉重、土地不足，城市工人储蓄有限，易受物价与动员政策冲击。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Japanese settlers, company employees, and some large landlords held relatively strong assets, while most Korean farming households were heavily indebted and land-poor; urban workers had limited savings and were vulnerable to price changes and mobilization measures.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民地资本主义与国家统制经济并存。大型企业、殖民银行和总督府主导投资方向，市场机制存在但越来越服从战时配给、价格管制、产业统制与帝国优先配置。
economicSystem.en: The economy combined colonial capitalism with state-directed controls. Large firms, colonial banks, and the Government-General dominated investment priorities; market mechanisms still existed but were increasingly subordinated to wartime rationing, price controls, industrial regulation, and imperial priority allocation.

source.zh: 直接税与土地相关税收

source.en: Enterprise profits, fees, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 总督府财政依赖税收、专卖和铁路收益，并与日本本土财政金融体系紧密相连。账面储备尚可，但战时统制与扩张政策使其流动性和自主性受东京决策强烈影响。
treasuryDescription.en: The Government-General's finances relied on taxes, monopoly income, and railway receipts, while remaining tightly linked to the fiscal and financial system of Japan proper. Book reserves were respectable, but wartime controls and expansion policies meant that liquidity and autonomy were strongly shaped by decisions from Tokyo.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以殖民地收支平衡、基础设施扩张和帝国战时动员为核心；表面上强调开发与现代化，实质上优先服务治安维稳、资源征发和对日经济整合。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on colonial budget management, infrastructure expansion, and imperial wartime mobilization. It outwardly emphasized development and modernization, but in practice prioritized internal security, resource extraction, and economic integration with Japan.

name.zh: 驻朝日本陆军守备部队

description.zh: 负责半岛防卫、港口与铁路枢纽警备、对满洲和华北后勤支援，以及关键工业区驻防
description.en: Responsible for defense of the peninsula, guarding ports and rail hubs, supporting logistics toward Manchuria and North China, and stationing troops in key industrial areas

description.zh: 承担军纪维持、反间谍、思想侦缉、交通线控制与政治镇压任务
description.en: Maintained discipline, conducted counterintelligence, ideological policing, line-of-communication control, and political repression

name.zh: 殖民警察与准军事治安部队
name.en: Colonial police and paramilitary internal security forces

description.zh: 遍布城乡的武装警察系统，是镇压民族运动、监控社会与执行战时动员的核心力量
description.en: The armed police system spread across towns and villages and was the core force for suppressing nationalist movements, monitoring society, and enforcing wartime mobilization

name.zh: 后备、训练与劳务动员体系
name.en: Reserve, training, and labor mobilization system

description.zh: 包括预备役编制、训练机关、后方警备与可转用于军事工程和运输的动员人员
description.en: Included reserve formations, training establishments, rear-area security, and mobilized personnel who could be shifted to military engineering and transport

commanderInChief.zh: 朝鲜总督兼受东京大本营与陆军省统制
commanderInChief.en: Governor-General of Korea under the authority of Tokyo's Imperial General Headquarters and Army Ministry

technology.zh: 驻朝部队装备与日本帝国陆军大体一致，拥有步枪、机枪、野炮、铁路运输、电话电报通信和有限装甲车辆支援；北部工业区与港口具备较强后勤能力，但朝鲜并非帝国最先进军事技术部署的首要地区。
technology.en: Forces in Korea were equipped broadly in line with the Imperial Japanese Army, including rifles, machine guns, field artillery, railway transport, telegraph and telephone communications, and limited armored support. Northern industrial zones and ports provided strong logistical capacity, though Korea was not the primary deployment area for the empire's most advanced military technology.

threats.zh: 主要威胁并非半岛立即遭受外部入侵，而是来自中日战争扩大背景下的战略后勤压力、苏联远东力量的潜在威慑、朝鲜民族独立运动、地下社会主义活动，以及战时总动员引发的社会不稳。
threats.en: The chief threats were not an immediate foreign invasion of the peninsula but strategic logistical pressures from the expanding Sino-Japanese War, the latent deterrent posed by Soviet Far Eastern forces, Korean independence movements, underground socialist activity, and social instability generated by total wartime mobilization.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气尚可，常规水平
description.en: Adequate morale, average cohesion

description.zh: 斯图卡/SBD俯冲轰炸机，精确打击地面目标
description.en: Stuka/SBD dive bomber for precise ground target strikes

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

description.zh: U型潜艇/美军舰队潜艇，破交战利器
description.en: U-boat/Fleet submarine for commerce raiding

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

populationDescription.zh: 1939年朝鲜人口约2300万，绝大多数为朝鲜人，另有数十万日本移民、官员、商人及其家属，少量中国人与其他侨民集中于城市和商业节点。人口分布南部农业区较密，北部工业区与矿区增长较快。
populationDescription.en: Korea's population in 1939 was about 23 million, overwhelmingly Korean, with several hundred thousand Japanese settlers, officials, merchants, and their families, plus small Chinese and other foreign communities concentrated in cities and commercial nodes. Population density was higher in the southern agricultural regions, while northern industrial and mining districts were growing rapidly.

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括日本殖民官僚与企业管理层、日本移民中产、朝鲜大地主与亲日协力者、朝鲜城市专业人士与小商人、产业工人、佃农、自耕农、矿工、渔民及贫困流动人口。殖民统治使民族身份与阶级地位高度重叠。
socialClasses.en: Social classes included Japanese colonial bureaucrats and corporate managers, a Japanese settler middle class, Korean large landlords and pro-Japanese collaborators, Korean urban professionals and petty merchants, industrial workers, tenant farmers, owner-cultivators, miners, fishermen, and impoverished migrants. Under colonial rule, ethnic identity and class status overlapped to a high degree.

allies.zh: 无独立外交；对外关系完全隶属于日本帝国，因此事实上与日本及其盟友、附属政权保持一致，包括满洲国以及与日本合作的中国占领区政权。
allies.en: No independent diplomacy; foreign relations were entirely subordinated to the Empire of Japan, and therefore aligned in practice with Japan and its associated allies and client regimes, including Manchukuo and collaborationist administrations in occupied China.

enemies.zh: 无独立宣战权；作为日本统治下领地，其敌对对象等同于日本在东亚的战争对手，尤其是国民政府、中国共产党控制区及潜在的苏联远东力量，同时也面对海外朝鲜独立运动网络的持续反抗。
enemies.en: It possessed no independent war-making power; as territory under Japanese rule, its enemies were effectively those of Japan in East Asia, especially the Nationalist government, Chinese Communist-controlled areas, and potentially Soviet Far Eastern forces, while also confronting continuing resistance from overseas Korean independence networks.

foreignPolicy.zh: 不存在自主外交政策。朝鲜在帝国战略中被定位为日本本土与满洲、中国战场之间的兵站、工业基地、农粮供给区和治安纵深，所有对外事务均由东京决定。
foreignPolicy.en: There was no autonomous foreign policy. In imperial strategy Korea was positioned as a logistical bridge, industrial base, grain supply zone, and security hinterland between Japan proper, Manchuria, and the China front; all external affairs were determined in Tokyo.

era.zh: 世界大战时代的殖民工业化阶段
era.en: Colonial industrialization stage of the World War Era

keyInnovations.zh: 大型铁路网、港口现代化、水力发电、化肥与化学工业、北部重工业综合体、现代矿业设备、电话电报网络、殖民统计与户籍管理体系，以及战时动员下的工业统制技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key developments included an extensive railway network, modernized ports, hydroelectric power, fertilizer and chemical industries, northern heavy-industry complexes, modern mining equipment, telephone and telegraph networks, colonial statistical and household-registration systems, and industrial control methods under wartime mobilization.

strengths.zh: 行政控制力强，铁路和港口发达，北部工业与电力基础较好，农业产出较高，并且与日本帝国市场、金融与军需体系紧密连接。
strengths.en: Its strengths were strong administrative control, well-developed railways and ports, a relatively solid northern industrial and power base, substantial agricultural output, and close integration with the imperial Japanese market, finance, and military supply system.

weaknesses.zh: 缺乏政治合法性，民族反抗持续，财富分配极不平等，经济结构依附于日本需求，农村贫困与社会压迫严重，文化同化政策激化长期不满。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a lack of political legitimacy, continuing national resistance, highly unequal wealth distribution, an economy dependent on Japanese demand, severe rural poverty and social oppression, and assimilation policies that deepened long-term resentment.

outlook.zh: 就1939年而言，殖民统治表面稳定且具备较高动员能力，但其稳定建立在警察镇压、战争扩张与帝国权力之上。若日本在更大战争中受挫，朝鲜的统治结构将迅速面临合法性崩解、民族独立运动高涨与权力重组。
outlook.en: As of 1939, colonial rule appeared stable and highly capable of mobilization, but that stability rested on police repression, wartime expansion, and imperial power. If Japan were to suffer setbacks in a larger war, Korea's governing structure would quickly face a collapse of legitimacy, rising nationalist movements, and major political realignment.

description.zh: 朝鲜半岛在1939年完全受日本殖民统治。
description.en: The Korean Peninsula was fully under Japanese colonial rule in 1939.

name.en: British India and the Princely States

ruler.zh: 乔治六世与林利斯戈侯爵
ruler.en: George VI and the Marquess of Linlithgow

rulerTitle.en: Emperor of India and Viceroy of India

dynasty.zh: 温莎王朝与英国殖民行政当局
dynasty.en: House of Windsor and British colonial administration

governmentForm.zh: 英国帝国殖民总督统治下的直辖省份与半自治土邦并存的复合帝国体制
governmentForm.en: Composite imperial system of directly ruled British provinces and semi-autonomous princely states under British imperial viceregal rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构高度分层，由英国殖民官僚与欧洲人居于顶层，其下为王公土邦统治者、地主、商人、专业中产阶层、城市工人、农民，以及受种姓、宗教、地区与部族差异深刻影响的广大底层群体。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly stratified, with British colonial officials and Europeans at the top, followed by princely rulers, landlords, merchants, professional middle classes, urban workers, and a vast rural peasantry deeply shaped by caste, religion, region, and tribal distinctions.

rulingClass.zh: 英国印度文官体系、高级军政官员、总督行政会议成员、土邦诸王公与大地主精英
rulingClass.en: The Indian Civil Service, senior military and executive officials, members of the Viceroy's Executive Council, princely rulers, and large landlord elites

succession.zh: 皇位依温莎王朝英国王位继承法传承；总督由英国君主任命；各土邦按各自王朝继承传统，在英国宗主权监督下承袭。
succession.en: The imperial crown followed the succession laws of the House of Windsor; the Viceroy was appointed by the British monarch; princely states followed their own dynastic succession customs under British paramountcy.

structure.zh: 政府由驻新德里的总督及其行政会议统领，下辖中央秘书处、各省总督或省长政府，以及通过政治代理人监督的数百个土邦。1935年《印度政府法》扩大了省级自治，但国防、外交、财政关键领域仍牢牢掌握在英国手中。
structure.en: The government was headed by the Viceroy and his Executive Council in New Delhi, supported by the central secretariat, provincial governments led by governors or premiers, and hundreds of princely states supervised through political agents. The Government of India Act of 1935 expanded provincial autonomy, but defence, foreign affairs, and core finance remained firmly in British hands.

function.zh: 负责治安、警察、监狱、政治监控、新闻审查与内部安全
function.en: Handled public order, policing, prisons, political surveillance, press controls, and internal security

function.zh: 负责预算、税收、关税、货币、债务与财政监督
function.en: Managed budgeting, taxation, customs, currency, debt, and fiscal supervision

function.zh: 负责英印军、军需、边防与战时动员协调
function.en: Oversaw the Indian Army, military supply, frontier defence, and wartime mobilization coordination

function.zh: 管理铁路网络、港口衔接、运输调度与战略后勤
function.en: Administered railways, port connections, transport scheduling, and strategic logistics

function.zh: 负责农业统计、粮食、灌溉、出口作物与工商管理
function.en: Handled agricultural statistics, food matters, irrigation, export crops, and commercial administration

function.zh: 管理学校、大学、公共卫生、工厂监管与劳资事务
function.en: Managed schools, universities, public health, factory regulation, and labour affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政分为英属省份、酋长省、专员辖区及土邦体系。省以下设专区、县、塔卢卡或乡镇，由印度文官、地方官与传统中介共同治理。土邦内部拥有不同程度自治，但其对外关系、防务与重大继承问题受英国控制。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was divided among British provinces, chief commissioners' provinces, special frontier areas, and the princely-state system. Below the provinces were divisions, districts, taluks, and municipalities administered by civil servants, local officers, and traditional intermediaries. Princely states retained varying degrees of internal autonomy, but external relations, defence, and major succession matters were controlled by Britain.

legalSystem.zh: 法律制度为英国普通法、成文殖民法规与宗教个人法并存的混合法体制。刑事、商事和行政领域深受英国法影响，而婚姻、继承等事项常依印度教法、伊斯兰法或地方习惯处理。各土邦司法制度差异很大。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was a hybrid of British common law, codified colonial statutes, and religious personal law. Criminal, commercial, and administrative matters were heavily shaped by British legal principles, while marriage and inheritance often followed Hindu law, Islamic law, or local custom. Judicial arrangements varied widely across the princely states.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地收入、盐税遗留体系、消费税、关税、铁路收益、所得税与企业税为主。农村税负依地区而异，部分地区仍依赖地主、包税人或传统土地中介征收。中央财政对关税和交通收益依赖较大。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied mainly on land revenue, remnants of the salt-tax structure, excise duties, customs, railway receipts, income tax, and corporate taxation. Rural burdens varied by region, and some areas still depended on landlords, revenue farmers, or traditional intermediaries for assessment and collection. The central budget depended heavily on customs and transport receipts.

religion.zh: 宗教极为多元，主要包括印度教、伊斯兰教、锡克教、佛教、耆那教、基督教、祆教与各类部族信仰。宗教身份在政治动员、社会秩序和选举制度中具有巨大作用。
religion.en: Religion was highly diverse, with Hinduism, Islam, Sikhism, Buddhism, Jainism, Christianity, Zoroastrianism, and numerous tribal faiths all present. Religious identity played a major role in political mobilization, social organization, and electoral arrangements.

philosophy.zh: 思想领域并存印度古典哲学、伊斯兰学术传统、英国自由主义、民族主义、社会主义、甘地主义非暴力思想以及穆斯林政治分离主义。现代政治理念与传统伦理在公共生活中持续交织。
philosophy.en: The intellectual landscape combined classical Indian philosophy, Islamic scholarly traditions, British liberalism, nationalism, socialism, Gandhian nonviolence, and Muslim political separatist thought. Modern political ideas and traditional ethical systems remained deeply intertwined in public life.

writingSystem.zh: 书写体系极其多样，通行文字包括英文字母、天城文、波斯-阿拉伯字母、孟加拉文、古木基文、泰米尔文、泰卢固文、卡纳达文、古吉拉特文、奥里亚文、马拉雅拉姆文等。
writingSystem.en: Writing systems were extremely diverse, including the Latin alphabet, Devanagari, Perso-Arabic script, Bengali script, Gurmukhi, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Gujarati, Odia, Malayalam, and others.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1939年前的英属印度已形成高度活跃的现代公共文化：大学教育扩展，印刷媒体与新闻业繁荣，孟买和加尔各答电影工业快速发展，文学上出现泰戈尔、伊克巴尔等重要人物，建筑上融合帝国古典风格与印度元素的新德里行政区成为殖民权力的象征。
culturalAchievements.en: By 1939, British India had developed a highly active modern public culture: university education had expanded, print media and journalism flourished, the film industries of Bombay and Calcutta were growing rapidly, literature had produced major figures such as Tagore and Iqbal, and the imperial-classical yet Indianized architecture of New Delhi stood as a symbol of colonial power.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于印欧语系印度-雅利安语支与达罗毗荼语系，另有汉藏语系、南亚语系及众多部族语言；英语则为统治、法律、高等教育与跨地区行政的重要语言。
languageFamily.en: The main languages belonged to the Indo-European Indo-Aryan branch and the Dravidian family, alongside Tibeto-Burman, Austroasiatic, and many tribal languages; English served as a key language of rule, law, higher education, and interregional administration.

gdpDescription.zh: 英属印度经济总量庞大，但人均产出偏低，呈现典型殖民地经济特征：人口众多、农业占主导、工业化集中于少数地区、基础设施服务于税收和对外贸易。1939年战争爆发前，经济总体稳定但结构脆弱，易受全球价格与季风波动影响。
gdpDescription.en: British India had a very large aggregate economy but low per capita output, showing classic colonial characteristics: a huge population, agricultural predominance, industrialization concentrated in a few regions, and infrastructure oriented toward revenue extraction and overseas trade. On the eve of war in 1939, the economy was broadly stable but structurally fragile and sensitive to world prices and monsoon fluctuations.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、棉纺织、黄麻、茶叶、煤炭、铁路运输、港口贸易、钢铁、采矿、糖业、皮革与轻工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, cotton textiles, jute, tea, coal, rail transport, port commerce, steel, mining, sugar, leather, and light manufacturing

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以黄麻制品、棉纱棉布、茶叶、稻米、皮革、锰矿、云母和部分钢铁产品为主；进口以机械、工业设备、车辆、化学品、石油制品与部分制成品为主。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included jute manufactures, cotton yarn and cloth, tea, rice, leather, manganese ore, mica, and some steel products; imports consisted mainly of machinery, industrial equipment, vehicles, chemicals, petroleum products, and selected manufactured goods.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差距极大。少数王公、地主、商人和工业家拥有显著土地、珠宝与金融资产，而绝大多数农村家庭仅有有限土地、牲畜和简陋居所，且常背负债务。城市中产阶层虽在扩张，但规模仍小。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. A small minority of princes, landlords, merchants, and industrialists held substantial land, jewelry, and financial assets, while most rural households possessed only limited land, livestock, and modest dwellings, often burdened by debt. The urban middle class was growing but remained relatively small.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为殖民地市场经济与国家主导基础设施体系并存。私营商贸和地主农业占主导，铁路、财政、对外关税与货币制度由殖民国家严格控制，战时动员开始增强政府对生产与贸易的干预。
economicSystem.en: The economic system combined a colonial market economy with state-directed infrastructure. Private commerce and landlord-based agriculture predominated, while railways, public finance, external tariffs, and currency were tightly controlled by the colonial state; wartime mobilization was beginning to increase government intervention in production and trade.

source.zh: 消费税与盐、酒、鸦片等专卖相关收入
source.en: Excise and monopoly-related revenues including salt, liquor, and opium
Posts, telegraphs, stamps, and other sources

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库依托印度储备银行、政府现金余额与伦敦金融联系维持。财政表面稳健，但对帝国金融体系、外贸收益和战时借款能力有高度依赖。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was supported by the Reserve Bank of India, government cash balances, and financial links with London. Fiscal conditions appeared sound on the surface, but remained highly dependent on the imperial financial system, trade earnings, and wartime borrowing capacity.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体偏保守，重视预算平衡、债务信誉和维持卢比稳定。1939年战争开始后，政策逐步转向军事支出扩张、控制进口与更强的中央财政动员。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, emphasizing balanced budgets, debt credibility, and rupee stability. After the outbreak of war in 1939, policy increasingly shifted toward expanded military spending, import controls, and stronger central fiscal mobilization.

description.zh: 1939年核心武装力量，由英军部队与印度籍部队组成，负责西北边境、国内驻防与海外帝国作战准备。
description.en: The main armed force in 1939, composed of British and Indian units, responsible for the North-West Frontier, internal garrison duties, and preparation for imperial overseas warfare.

description.zh: 包括辅助军、边防部队、准军事警备力量与各土邦部队，训练和装备水平差异显著，战时可用于守备、后勤与地方防务。
description.en: Included auxiliary formations, frontier troops, paramilitary security forces, and princely state contingents, with widely varying training and equipment; in wartime they could serve in garrison, logistics, and local defence roles.

description.zh: 规模较小，主要承担近海巡逻、扫雷、护航与港口防务，受皇家海军主导。
description.en: Small in size, it mainly handled coastal patrol, minesweeping, convoy escort, and harbour defence under broader Royal Navy direction.

description.zh: 处于早期发展阶段，规模有限，主要执行侦察、联络与支援任务，战时将迅速扩编。
description.en: Still in an early stage of development, limited in size and focused on reconnaissance, liaison, and support duties, though it was expected to expand rapidly in wartime.

name.zh: 宪兵、武装警察与边境警备
name.en: Military police, armed police, and frontier security forces

description.zh: 用于内部秩序维持、交通线保护、边境巡逻与镇压骚乱，在殖民统治中作用重要。
description.en: Used for maintaining internal order, protecting lines of communication, patrolling borderlands, and suppressing unrest, they were vital to colonial rule.

commanderInChief.zh: 印度总督为名义最高统帅，实际军事指挥由印度总司令罗伯特·卡塞尔斯将军承担
commanderInChief.en: The Viceroy was the nominal supreme authority, while actual military command was exercised by General Sir Robert Cassels, Commander-in-Chief, India

technology.zh: 军队技术处于两次大战之间的过渡阶段，装备以步枪、机枪、山炮、野战炮、装甲车、少量坦克、双翼机与早期单翼机为主。其铁路动员、后勤组织与山地边境作战经验较强，但重型机械化和现代空海力量不足。
technology.en: Military technology stood at an interwar transitional level, with rifles, machine guns, mountain and field artillery, armoured cars, a small number of tanks, biplanes, and early monoplanes. It possessed strong railway mobilization, logistics, and mountain-frontier warfare experience, but lacked heavy mechanization and modern air-sea power.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西北边境动荡、阿富汗方向不确定性、印度洋与东南亚战局扩大的可能、日本帝国在亚洲的推进、以及国内民族主义运动、宗教紧张和局部骚乱。1939年欧洲战争爆发后，印度被迅速纳入英国全球战争体系。
threats.en: Main threats came from instability on the North-West Frontier, uncertainty relating to Afghanistan, the possibility of war spreading across the Indian Ocean and Southeast Asia, Japanese imperial expansion in Asia, and internal nationalist agitation, communal tensions, and local unrest. After the outbreak of the European war in 1939, India was rapidly drawn into Britain's global war system.

description.zh: 精锐训练标准，实战演习频繁
description.en: Elite standards, frequent live exercises

description.zh: 士气较高，部队凝聚力良好
description.en: Fairly high morale, good unit cohesion

description.zh: 舰载机母舰，太平洋战争主角
description.en: Carrier launching aircraft, star of the Pacific War

description.zh: U型潜艇/美军舰队潜艇，破交战利器
description.en: U-boat/Fleet submarine for commerce raiding

description.zh: 喷火/零式/Bf109战斗机，争夺制空权
description.en: Spitfire/Zero/Bf109 fighter for air superiority

description.zh: 斯图卡/SBD俯冲轰炸机，精确打击地面目标
description.en: Stuka/SBD dive bomber for precise ground target strikes

description.zh: 世界首种弹道导弹，射程320公里
description.en: World's first ballistic missile with 320km range

populationDescription.zh: 人口约3.9亿，是世界上人口最稠密、社会构成最复杂的政治实体之一。人口绝大多数居住于农村，地区间在密度、语言、宗教、阶级和生活水平上差异巨大。
populationDescription.en: With about 390 million people, it was one of the world's most populous and socially complex political entities. The overwhelming majority lived in rural areas, and regional differences in density, language, religion, class, and living standards were immense.

description.zh: 莫卧儿帝国首都
description.en: Mughal Empire capital

description.zh: 莫卧儿帝国文化名城
description.en: Cultural city of the Mughal Empire

description.zh: 印度硅谷，IT与创新中心
description.en: India's Silicon Valley, IT and innovation hub

socialClasses.zh: 主要社会阶层包括英国官员与欧洲侨民、土邦王公、地主与扎明达尔、工商业资产阶级、受教育专业中产阶层、城市工人、店主与手工业者、无地农民、小农、佃农、债务农户、贱民与被压迫种姓群体，以及众多少数民族和部族社群。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included British officials and European settlers, princely rulers, landlords and zamindars, commercial and industrial bourgeois groups, educated professional middle classes, urban workers, shopkeepers and artisans, landless labourers, smallholders, tenants, indebted peasants, depressed castes and outcaste communities, and numerous ethnic and tribal populations.

allies.zh: 作为大英帝国组成部分，英属印度与英国本土、自治领及法国等反轴心国家站在同一阵营；并与阿富汗、伊朗、伊拉克、海湾酋长国等周边地区维持以帝国利益为导向的关系。
allies.en: As part of the British Empire, British India stood aligned with the United Kingdom, the Dominions, and anti-Axis states such as France; it also maintained relations with Afghanistan, Iran, Iraq, and the Gulf sheikhdoms in ways shaped by imperial interests.

enemies.zh: 1939年直接敌对核心为纳粹德国；随着亚洲战争扩大，日本帝国成为最重要的潜在敌人。内部层面，英国当局将激进民族主义、革命组织与边境叛乱力量视为安全威胁，但这些并非主权国家敌人。
enemies.en: In 1939 the principal direct enemy was Nazi Germany; as the Asian war widened, the Japanese Empire became the most significant potential adversary. Internally, the British authorities treated radical nationalism, revolutionary groups, and frontier insurgents as security threats, though these were not sovereign-state enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策并非由印度民选机构独立制定，而是服从英国帝国全球战略。其核心目标是保卫印度作为帝国财政、兵员和后勤基地的地位，维持印度洋航线安全，控制西北边境，并阻止敌对强权渗入南亚。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was not independently set by Indian representative institutions but subordinated to British global imperial strategy. Its core aims were to preserve India as a financial, manpower, and logistical base of empire, secure Indian Ocean routes, control the North-West Frontier, and prevent hostile great powers from penetrating South Asia.

era.zh: 世界大战时代

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术能力包括广阔铁路网、电报与电话行政通信、现代港口体系、钢铁和煤炭工业、军需装配、航空起步设施、灌溉工程以及现代大学与科研机构。尽管拥有塔塔钢铁等工业基础和相当规模工程能力，但先进机械制造、汽车、飞机与高端化工仍严重依赖进口或帝国技术体系。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological capacities included a vast railway network, telegraph and telephone administrative communications, modern port systems, steel and coal industries, ordnance assembly, early aviation infrastructure, irrigation works, and modern universities and research institutions. Despite industrial foundations such as Tata Steel and considerable engineering capacity, advanced machine building, automobiles, aircraft, and high-end chemicals still depended heavily on imports or the wider imperial technological system.

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于庞大人口、广阔领土、丰富农业和矿产资源、成熟铁路网络、连接中东与东亚的战略位置，以及可为英国战争提供巨量兵员、税收和补给。主要城市已形成初步工业基础和现代教育精英。
strengths.en: Its greatest strengths were its enormous population, vast territory, substantial agricultural and mineral resources, established railway network, strategic position linking the Middle East and East Asia, and ability to supply Britain with huge quantities of manpower, revenue, and matériel. Major cities had also developed initial industrial bases and modern educated elites.

weaknesses.zh: 核心弱点是殖民统治合法性不足、贫困普遍、农业脆弱、地区发展极不平衡、文盲率高、公共卫生薄弱、宗教与种姓矛盾尖锐，以及政治体制缺乏真正统一的主权与民主代表性。土邦体系也加剧了制度碎片化。
weaknesses.en: Its central weaknesses were the weak legitimacy of colonial rule, widespread poverty, fragile agriculture, highly uneven regional development, high illiteracy, poor public health, sharp communal and caste divisions, and a political system lacking genuine unified sovereignty and democratic representation. The princely-state structure also deepened institutional fragmentation.

outlook.zh: 1939年的前景表面稳定但内在紧张。短期内，战争将推动工业、财政与军事扩张，使印度在帝国体系中的地位更加重要；中长期看，民族独立运动、国大党与穆斯林联盟的竞争、以及战争造成的经济与政治压力，将使这一帝国结构越来越难以维持。
outlook.en: The outlook in 1939 was outwardly stable but internally tense. In the short term, war would stimulate industrial, fiscal, and military expansion and increase India's importance within the imperial system; in the medium to long term, the independence movement, rivalry between the Congress and the Muslim League, and the economic and political pressures of war would make the imperial structure increasingly difficult to sustain.

description.zh: 印度次大陆在1939年大部仍受英属印度统治，并包含众多半自治土邦。
description.en: Most of the Indian subcontinent in 1939 remained under British India, alongside numerous semi-autonomous princely states.

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权的君主立宪倾向王国，实际仍由国王与王室精英主导
governmentForm.en: A centralized kingdom with constitutional tendencies, though in practice still dominated by the king and royal elite

socialStructure.zh: 社会以部族、宗教、地方乡绅和王室官僚体系为核心，城市中存在规模较小的商人、军官与受教育阶层，农村人口占绝对多数
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on tribes, religion, local notables, and the royal bureaucratic order, with a small urban stratum of merchants, officers, and educated elites; the overwhelming majority lived in rural areas

rulingClass.zh: 巴拉克宰王室成员、宫廷贵族、高级军官、部族首领、乌里玛与省级官僚
rulingClass.en: The Barakzai royal family, court aristocracy, senior officers, tribal chiefs, ulema, and provincial officials

succession.zh: 王朝世袭，通常由巴拉克宰王室男性成员继承王位
succession.en: Hereditary dynastic succession, normally passing to a male member of the Barakzai royal house

structure.zh: 1939年的阿富汗政府由国王统领，首相和内阁负责日常行政，中央各部设于喀布尔，并通过省长、地区官员和与部族首领的协商来治理地方
structure.en: In 1939 the Afghan state was headed by the king, with the prime minister and cabinet handling day-to-day administration; central ministries in Kabul governed the provinces through governors, district officials, and negotiated relationships with tribal leaders

function.zh: 协调国家政策、宫廷事务、内阁运作与高级任命
function.en: Coordinated state policy, court affairs, cabinet operations, and senior appointments

function.zh: 处理对外关系、驻外使团、条约与中立政策
function.en: Handled foreign relations, diplomatic missions, treaties, and the policy of neutrality

function.zh: 管理地方行政、警务、治安、部族事务与国内秩序
function.en: Managed local administration, policing, public order, tribal affairs, and internal security

function.zh: 征税、预算编制、海关、国库和国家会计
function.en: Oversaw taxation, budgeting, customs, the treasury, and state accounting

function.zh: 管理陆军、征兵、后勤、军校与边防部署
function.en: Administered the army, recruitment, logistics, military schools, and frontier deployment

function.zh: 监督法院体系、国家法令与宗教法和习惯法之间的协调
function.en: Supervised the court system and coordinated state law with religious and customary law

function.zh: 管理学校、课程、师资培养与有限的现代化教育推广
function.en: Managed schools, curricula, teacher training, and the limited expansion of modern education

function.zh: 负责道路、灌溉、政府建筑及部分通信基础设施
function.en: Handled roads, irrigation, government buildings, and some communications infrastructure

function.zh: 监管内外贸易、边境关卡、进口许可与市场监督
function.en: Regulated domestic and foreign trade, frontier customs posts, import licensing, and market oversight

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省为基本单位，由中央任命的省长管理；县区层面权力有限，国家在偏远地区往往依赖部族长老、地方汗和宗教领袖协助维持秩序与征税
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized mainly through provinces governed by centrally appointed officials; district authority was limited, and in remote areas the state often depended on tribal elders, local khans, and religious leaders to maintain order and assist in tax collection

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系兼具伊斯兰教法、国家法令与部族习惯法。城市和政府控制较强地区更依赖国家法院，边疆与部族地区则常由习惯法与宗教裁判共同发挥作用
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic law, royal/state decrees, and tribal customary law. Urban and more tightly governed areas relied more on state courts, while frontier and tribal regions often saw customary and religious adjudication play major roles

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自关税、土地税、畜牧与农业征收、消费税以及国营或特许收入。国家征税能力有限，对边远地区更多依赖协商与间接征收
taxationSystem.en: State revenue came mainly from customs duties, land taxes, agricultural and livestock levies, excises, and state or concession income. Fiscal reach was limited, and in remote regions the government relied heavily on negotiation and indirect collection

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教占绝对主导地位，逊尼派为多数，什叶派少数族群亦具有重要存在；宗教机构对教育、司法与社会规范影响深远
religion.en: Islam was overwhelmingly dominant, with a Sunni majority and significant Shia minorities; religious institutions had deep influence over education, justice, and social norms

philosophy.zh: 政治思想以伊斯兰正统、王权合法性、部族荣誉观念和谨慎现代化相结合；政府在不激烈冲击传统结构的前提下推动有限改革
philosophy.en: Political thought combined Islamic orthodoxy, monarchical legitimacy, tribal codes of honor, and cautious modernization; the state sought limited reform without sharply disrupting traditional structures

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为阿拉伯字母改写的波斯文与普什图文；政府、文学与教育中波斯语地位尤其重要，普什图语国家地位也在提升
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Perso-Arabic script used for Dari Persian and Pashto; Persian remained especially important in government, literature, and education, while Pashto was gaining greater national status

culturalAchievements.zh: 1930年代阿富汗在教育、国家象征、行政建设和城市建筑方面取得有限但重要进展。喀布尔形成了早期现代学校、报刊、政府机构和王室赞助的文化活动，但全国文化生活仍以地方传统、口述诗歌、宗教学习与部族习俗为主
culturalAchievements.en: By the 1930s Afghanistan had made limited but important gains in education, state symbolism, administration, and urban architecture. Kabul saw the growth of early modern schools, newspapers, government institutions, and court-sponsored cultural activity, while most of the country remained culturally rooted in local traditions, oral poetry, religious learning, and tribal custom

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属于伊朗语族，包括达里波斯语和普什图语，另有突厥语及其他少数民族语言
languageFamily.en: The main languages belonged to the Iranian branch, notably Dari Persian and Pashto, alongside Turkic and other minority languages

gdpDescription.zh: 阿富汗经济以自给农业、畜牧业和区域性商贸为主，人均产出较低，货币化程度有限。喀布尔及少数城市出现小规模制造业与国家推动的近代企业，但整体仍属欠发达农业经济
gdpDescription.en: Afghanistan's economy was based chiefly on subsistence agriculture, pastoralism, and regional commerce, with low per capita output and limited monetization. Kabul and a few other towns had small-scale manufacturing and state-backed modern enterprises, but the country remained an underdeveloped agrarian economy overall

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、地毯与纺织手工业、皮革加工、干果生产、地方性采矿、建筑与运输服务
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, pastoralism, carpet and textile handicrafts, leather working, dried fruit production, localized mining, construction, and transport services

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以羊毛、羔皮、地毯、干果、药材和部分牲畜产品为主；进口以纺织品、金属制品、机械、糖、茶、石油制品和军需品为主
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on wool, karakul pelts, carpets, dried fruits, medicinal products, and some livestock goods; imports included textiles, metal goods, machinery, sugar, tea, petroleum products, and military supplies

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要以土地、牲畜、粮食储备、地毯、首饰和家族关系网络形式保存，城市中的现金储蓄与商业资本较少且集中于商人和官僚阶层
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was held mainly in land, livestock, grain stores, carpets, jewelry, and kinship networks; urban cash savings and commercial capital were limited and concentrated among merchants and officials

economicSystem.zh: 以传统农业和地方市场为基础的混合经济，国家在财政、关税、少数工业项目和基础设施上发挥有限但上升中的作用
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy grounded in traditional agriculture and local markets, with a limited but growing state role in finance, customs, selected industries, and infrastructure

source.zh: 邮政、司法费及其他杂项
source.en: Postal, judicial fees, and other miscellaneous income

category.zh: 财政储备与债务相关支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 阿富汗国库规模不大，但在1930年代后期相对稳健。国家努力维持预算平衡、谨慎支出并保有一定现金与贵金属储备，以应对边疆危机和进口需求
treasuryDescription.en: The Afghan treasury was modest but comparatively sound in the late 1930s. The state aimed to preserve budget balance, spend cautiously, and keep some cash and bullion reserves to meet frontier crises and import needs

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体保守，强调平衡预算、依赖关税收入、控制现代化支出节奏，并避免因过快改革而刺激地方反弹
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was broadly conservative, emphasizing balanced budgets, reliance on customs revenue, controlled pacing of modernization spending, and avoidance of reforms so rapid that they might provoke local backlash

description.zh: 国家武装核心，主要由步兵、骑兵残余编制、山地部队、炮兵和边防驻军组成，装备水平不一，训练较为有限
description.en: The core of the state's armed forces, composed mainly of infantry, residual cavalry formations, mountain troops, artillery, and frontier garrisons, with uneven equipment and limited training

description.zh: 负责国内治安、交通线守备、城市秩序和协助地方行政，在国家统治薄弱地区尤为重要
description.en: Responsible for internal security, guarding communications lines, maintaining urban order, and supporting local administration, especially important where state authority was weak

description.zh: 保护国王、宫廷和首都要地，政治可靠性通常高于普通部队
description.en: Protected the king, the court, and key sites in the capital, and were generally considered more politically reliable than ordinary troops

description.zh: 规模很小，拥有少量教练机、运输机和轻型军机，主要用于联络、侦察和象征性现代化展示
description.en: Very small in size, with a limited number of trainers, transports, and light military aircraft used mainly for liaison, reconnaissance, and symbolic modernization

commanderInChief.zh: 穆罕默德·查希尔·沙阿国王（名义上）；实际军政协调由首相与战争部主持
commanderInChief.en: King Mohammed Zahir Shah (nominally); practical military coordination was conducted by the prime minister and Ministry of War

technology.zh: 军队技术水平落后于欧洲强国和苏联、英属印度等邻近大国，武器以步枪、机枪、山炮和少量旧式火炮为主，机动车、无线电和航空力量都十分有限
technology.en: Military technology lagged far behind the European powers and nearby major states such as the Soviet Union and British India; weaponry centered on rifles, machine guns, mountain guns, and small numbers of older artillery pieces, while motorization, radio communications, and air power were all very limited

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自大国夹缝中的战略脆弱性、边境部族动荡、与英属印度边境的长期摩擦，以及苏联与英国势力在中亚和南亚的潜在压力。1939年欧洲战争爆发后，阿富汗更重视维持中立与边疆安全
threats.en: The principal threats came from strategic vulnerability between great powers, unrest among frontier tribes, longstanding friction along the border with British India, and the potential pressure of Soviet and British influence in Central and South Asia. After the outbreak of war in Europe in 1939, Afghanistan placed even greater emphasis on neutrality and frontier security

description.zh: 初步正规化，有季节性操练
description.en: Semi-professional, seasonal drills

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: 破片手榴弹，步兵必备
description.en: Fragmentation hand grenade, infantry essential

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

description.zh: 75-105mm榴弹炮，师级火力支援
description.en: 75-105mm howitzer, division-level fire support

populationDescription.zh: 阿富汗人口约1300万，民族、语言和宗教社群多样，主要包括普什图人、塔吉克人、哈扎拉人、乌兹别克人及其他山区和边疆族群；人口大多分散在农村、山谷和绿洲地区
populationDescription.en: Afghanistan's population was about 13 million, diverse in ethnicity, language, and sect, including Pashtuns, Tajiks, Hazaras, Uzbeks, and other mountain and frontier communities; most people were dispersed across rural districts, valleys, and oasis settlements

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室与贵族、部族首领、宗教学者、地主和地方名流、城市商人与手工业者、小规模官僚和军官群体，以及占绝大多数的农民、牧民和佃户
socialClasses.en: Social strata included the royal family and aristocracy, tribal chiefs, religious scholars, landlords and local notables, urban merchants and artisans, a small bureaucracy and officer corps, and the overwhelming majority of peasants, pastoralists, and tenants

allies.zh: 阿富汗1939年没有正式军事同盟，主要与英国、苏联、伊朗、土耳其、德国、意大利和日本等国保持务实外交联系，同时尽量避免依附任何单一强权
allies.en: Afghanistan had no formal military allies in 1939; it maintained pragmatic diplomatic relations with Britain, the Soviet Union, Iran, Turkey, Germany, Italy, and Japan while trying to avoid dependence on any single great power

enemies.zh: 无公开宣战敌国，但对任何可能侵犯其独立的大国干涉均保持警惕；与英属印度边境部族问题构成持续紧张来源
enemies.en: It had no openly declared wartime enemies, but remained wary of interference by any great power that might threaten its independence; tribal issues along the frontier with British India were a continuing source of tension

foreignPolicy.zh: 阿富汗奉行谨慎中立、平衡列强和维护独立的外交路线。其核心目标是在英国与苏联之间充当缓冲国，通过有限开放与多边接触争取援助、贸易和军事技术，同时避免卷入第二次世界大战初期的阵营对抗
foreignPolicy.en: Afghanistan pursued a cautious foreign policy of neutrality, balancing the great powers and preserving independence. Its central aim was to remain a buffer between Britain and the Soviet Union, using limited openness and diversified contacts to secure aid, trade, and military know-how while avoiding entanglement in the early alignments of the Second World War

era.zh: 世界大战时代

keyInnovations.zh: 阿富汗在1939年的技术发展主要体现为国家主导的有限现代化，包括基础公路建设、汽车运输在行政和军事中的扩大使用、无线电通信、少量航空训练、近代学校体系和部分机械化工厂。总体上技术扩散局限于首都和少数城市
keyInnovations.en: Afghanistan's technological development in 1939 was marked by limited state-led modernization, including basic road construction, increased use of motor transport in administration and the military, radio communications, small-scale aviation training, modern schools, and a few mechanized factories. Overall, technological diffusion remained largely confined to the capital and a handful of towns

strengths.zh: 阿富汗的优势在于成功保持独立、地理位置有利于平衡列强、王朝统治在1930年代后期相对稳固、国家财政较谨慎且社会对外来统治普遍警惕，增强了抵御直接吞并的能力
strengths.en: Afghanistan's strengths lay in its successful preservation of independence, its position as a useful buffer between great powers, the relative consolidation of dynastic rule in the late 1930s, prudent state finances, and a strong social resistance to foreign domination that made direct annexation difficult

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点包括经济落后、交通和通信薄弱、行政渗透不足、教育水平低、民族与部族结构复杂、军备有限且工业基础狭窄，这些都限制了国家动员和改革能力
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included economic backwardness, poor transport and communications, shallow administrative penetration, low educational attainment, complex ethnic and tribal structures, limited armament, and a narrow industrial base, all of which constrained state mobilization and reform

outlook.zh: 1939年的阿富汗整体前景稳定但脆弱。只要王室继续维持财政克制、部族妥协和外交中立，王国有望在大战初期避免重大冲击；但若外部强权竞争加剧或国内改革失衡，稳定局面仍可能迅速恶化
outlook.en: Afghanistan's outlook in 1939 was stable but fragile. If the monarchy continued to maintain fiscal restraint, tribal compromise, and diplomatic neutrality, the kingdom could likely avoid major shocks in the early war years; however, intensified great-power rivalry or poorly balanced domestic reform could quickly undermine that stability

description.zh: 阿富汗在1939年维持独立王国地位，处于英苏之间的缓冲带。
description.en: Afghanistan remained an independent kingdom in 1939, serving as a buffer between British and Soviet spheres.

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权的君主立宪制，实际上由沙阿主导的威权现代化国家
governmentForm.en: A centralized constitutional monarchy, in practice an authoritarian modernizing state dominated by the Shah

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷集团、军官团、中央官僚、城市地主与商人、乌理玛与宗教机构、部族首领、手工业者、工人和广大农民组成；国家在1930年代持续削弱部族自治与传统地方权力，以强化中央控制。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the royal court, military officers, central bureaucrats, urban landlords and merchants, the ulama and religious institutions, tribal chiefs, artisans, workers, and a large peasantry; during the 1930s the state steadily weakened tribal autonomy and traditional local power in order to strengthen central control.

rulingClass.zh: 巴列维王室、军方高层、经沙阿提拔的新式官僚精英，以及与国家关系密切的大地主和城市上层
rulingClass.en: The Pahlavi royal house, senior military leadership, a new bureaucratic elite elevated by the Shah, and major landholders and urban notables closely tied to the state

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，按巴列维王朝父系继承；1939年王储为穆罕默德·礼萨·巴列维
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy following patrilineal succession within the Pahlavi dynasty; in 1939 the heir apparent was Mohammad Reza Pahlavi

structure.zh: 国家名义上依据1906年宪法和国民议会运作，但1939年的实际权力集中于沙阿、宫廷、陆军总参谋系统以及忠于王权的内阁。地方行政由省、县和区组成，中央政府通过任命官员、驻军、宪兵与警察实施控制。
structure.en: The state nominally operated under the 1906 constitution and the National Consultative Assembly, but in 1939 real power was concentrated in the Shah, the court, the army high command, and cabinets loyal to the crown. Provincial administration was organized through provinces, counties, and districts, with central control exercised through appointed officials, army garrisons, the gendarmerie, and police.

name.en: Court and Office of the Shah

function.zh: 协调王室命令、人事任免、国家礼仪以及对内阁和军队高层的直接监督
function.en: Coordinated royal directives, senior appointments, state ceremony, and direct oversight of the cabinet and high military command

function.zh: 管理省级行政、地方官员、治安、人口登记与地方选举监督
function.en: Managed provincial administration, local officials, public order, population registration, and oversight of local elections

function.zh: 负责税收征收、预算编制、国库管理、关税和国家垄断收入
function.en: Handled tax collection, budget preparation, treasury management, customs, and state monopoly revenues

function.zh: 推进法院世俗化、统一法制、监督法官与检察体系
function.en: Advanced secularization of the courts, unified the legal system, and supervised judges and prosecutors

function.zh: 负责陆军建制、军官任命、兵役征集、后勤和军备采购
function.en: Oversaw army organization, officer appointments, conscription, logistics, and military procurement

function.zh: 处理对外关系、条约谈判、驻外使团与中立外交平衡
function.en: Managed foreign relations, treaty negotiations, overseas missions, and the balancing diplomacy of neutrality

function.zh: 建设铁路、公路、桥梁、邮政和电报网络，推动全国交通整合
function.en: Built railways, roads, bridges, postal services, and telegraph networks, advancing national integration

function.zh: 扩展国家学校体系、师范教育、教材审定和世俗教育推广
function.en: Expanded the state school system, teacher training, textbook supervision, and secular education

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为若干省份，由中央任命省长、县长及警务与财政官员。部族地区在1930年代被强制纳入常规行政体系，地方首领权力受到压制，驻军和宪兵承担维稳职能。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provinces administered by centrally appointed governors, county heads, and police and fiscal officials. Tribal regions were forcibly integrated into regular administration during the 1930s, with local chiefs curtailed and security maintained by army garrisons and the gendarmerie.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系处于从宗教法和习惯法向国家成文法过渡阶段。礼萨·沙阿时期大力扩展世俗法院、商法和刑法体系，同时在婚姻和继承等领域仍保留部分伊斯兰法影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was in transition from religious and customary law toward codified state law. Under Reza Shah, secular courts, commercial law, and criminal law were greatly expanded, though Islamic law retained influence in areas such as marriage and inheritance.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要来自海关、间接税、土地税、国家专卖、企业收费和石油特许收益。中央政府加强税务官僚体系，以减少地方截留并提高对农村与贸易的征收能力。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came chiefly from customs, indirect taxes, land taxation, state monopolies, administrative fees, and oil concession income. The central government strengthened the tax bureaucracy to reduce local diversion of funds and improve extraction from the countryside and commercial activity.

religion.zh: 以什叶派十二伊玛目派伊斯兰教为主，占绝对多数；同时存在逊尼派少数群体、亚美尼亚和亚述基督徒、犹太社群以及拜火教少数群体。国家虽不废除伊斯兰地位，但明显限制教士的社会和司法权力。
religion.en: Twelver Shi'a Islam was the dominant religion by a wide margin; there were also Sunni minorities, Armenian and Assyrian Christians, Jewish communities, and a small Zoroastrian population. The state did not abolish Islam’s public status, but it clearly restricted clerical social and judicial authority.

philosophy.zh: 官方思想强调伊朗民族主义、国家统一、世俗行政、秩序、军国化纪律与前伊斯兰波斯历史的复兴象征，同时吸收有限的欧洲国家主义与现代化观念。
philosophy.en: Official thought emphasized Iranian nationalism, national unity, secular administration, order, militarized discipline, and symbolic revival of pre-Islamic Persian history, while absorbing selected European ideas of statism and modernization.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用以阿拉伯字母为基础的波斯文字书写波斯语；政府、教育和报刊使用标准化现代波斯文。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was Persian written in a modified Arabic script; government, education, and the press used a standardized modern Persian orthography.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1930年代的重要文化成就包括德黑兰大学的发展、国家学校扩张、考古与博物馆事业推进、现代民族史叙述的塑造、铁路和公共建筑带来的国家象征景观，以及服饰与礼仪改革所体现的国家世俗化形象。
culturalAchievements.en: Major cultural achievements of the 1930s included the development of the University of Tehran, expansion of state schools, promotion of archaeology and museums, construction of a modern national historical narrative, national-symbolic landscapes created by railways and public buildings, and the secular image embodied in reforms of dress and public etiquette.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言为波斯语，属印欧语系伊朗语支；国内同时广泛使用阿塞拜疆突厥语、库尔德语、阿拉伯语、吉拉基语、俾路支语、亚美尼亚语等。
languageFamily.en: The official language was Persian, an Indo-European language of the Iranian branch; Azerbaijan Turkic, Kurdish, Arabic, Gilaki, Balochi, Armenian, and other languages were also widely spoken.

gdpDescription.zh: 1939年的伊朗经济仍以农业为主，但国家主导的铁路、公路、工业作坊、军工、纺织、制糖和行政集中化正在改变传统结构。石油出口构成最重要的现代外汇来源，但其收益受特许制度限制，国家整体仍属中低收入发展中经济体。
gdpDescription.en: Iran’s 1939 economy remained predominantly agrarian, but state-led railways, roads, industrial workshops, armaments, textiles, sugar, and administrative centralization were changing the traditional structure. Oil exports formed the most important modern source of foreign exchange, though state gains were limited by the concessionary system; overall the country remained a lower-middle-level developing economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 农业、畜牧业、石油开采与出口、地毯制造、纺织、制糖、烟草加工、交通建设、轻工业、军需工业
mainIndustries.en: Agriculture, animal husbandry, oil extraction and export, carpet production, textiles, sugar refining, tobacco processing, transport construction, light industry, and military supply industries

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以石油、地毯、棉花、干果、皮张和少量矿产为主；进口以机械、车辆、纺织品、金属制品、工业设备、药品和军需物资为主。
tradeGoods.en: Exports were led by oil, carpets, cotton, dried fruits, hides, and some minerals; imports included machinery, vehicles, textiles, metal goods, industrial equipment, pharmaceuticals, and military supplies.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异极大。城市地主、商人和与国家合作的官僚军官家庭积累了显著资产，而广大农村人口主要持有土地使用权、牲畜和简单生产工具，现金储蓄有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Urban landlords, merchants, and families of state-connected officials and officers held significant assets, while much of the rural population possessed mainly land-use rights, livestock, and simple productive tools, with little cash savings.

economicSystem.zh: 国家引导的混合经济。农业仍由地主—佃农关系主导，城市商业由私人商人和巴扎体系维系，而铁路、基础设施、军工和部分现代工厂受到国家直接推动与保护。
economicSystem.en: A state-guided mixed economy. Agriculture was still dominated by landlord-tenant relations, urban commerce by private merchants and the bazaar system, while railways, infrastructure, armaments, and parts of modern industry were directly promoted and protected by the state.

source.zh: 石油特许收益与相关付款
source.en: Oil concession revenues and related payments

source.zh: 国家专卖、收费与企业收入
source.en: State monopolies, fees, and enterprise income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况在地区国家中尚属稳健，得益于中央集权和税收扩张，但长期财政承压于军费、基础设施建设和工业化投资，且石油收益仍无法完全由国家掌控。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was comparatively solid by regional standards, owing to centralization and expanded taxation, but long-term finances were strained by military costs, infrastructure building, and industrialization, while oil revenues still remained only partly under state control.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以集中收入、扩大国家投资和维持军政机器为核心。政府倾向于把预算用于道路、铁路、军队、教育与官僚体系扩张，而非广泛社会福利。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy centered on concentrating revenues, enlarging state investment, and maintaining the military-administrative apparatus. The government preferred to direct budgets toward roads, railways, the army, education, and bureaucratic expansion rather than broad social welfare.

description.zh: 军队是礼萨·沙阿政权的核心支柱，承担国防、镇压部族叛乱、边境驻防和国家整合任务。装备以步兵武器、炮兵、少量装甲车辆和有限机械化运输为主。
description.en: The army was the core pillar of Reza Shah’s regime, responsible for national defense, suppression of tribal revolts, border garrisoning, and national integration. Its equipment centered on infantry arms, artillery, a small number of armored vehicles, and limited motorized transport.

name.en: Imperial Gendarmerie and Internal Security Forces

description.zh: 负责乡村治安、交通线保护、税收协助和地方控制，在偏远省份常与正规军协同行动。
description.en: Responsible for rural security, protection of communication lines, assistance in tax enforcement, and local control; in remote provinces it often operated alongside the regular army.

description.zh: 海军规模很小，主要部署于波斯湾和里海沿岸，任务是港口巡逻、海关协助与有限海防。
description.en: The navy was very small, deployed chiefly in the Persian Gulf and along the Caspian coast, with duties of port patrol, customs support, and limited coastal defense.

description.zh: 空军处于初步建设阶段，拥有教练机、侦察机和少量轻型作战飞机，主要用于通信、侦察和象征性现代军力展示。
description.en: The air force was in an early stage of development, possessing trainers, reconnaissance aircraft, and a small number of light combat planes, mainly for communications, reconnaissance, and symbolic display of modern military power.

commanderInChief.zh: 礼萨·沙阿·巴列维
commanderInChief.en: Reza Shah Pahlavi

technology.zh: 军备水平在中东地区属中等偏上，但明显落后于欧洲列强和苏联。武器来源多样，部分依赖欧洲进口；后勤、机械化、通信、装甲和防空能力有限，难以抵御大国联合入侵。
technology.en: Military capability was above average for the Middle East, but clearly behind the major European powers and the Soviet Union. Arms sources were diverse and partly dependent on European imports; logistics, mechanization, communications, armor, and air defense were limited, making the country unable to resist a coordinated great-power invasion.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方的苏联、南方和海上的英国力量，以及大战扩张背景下伊朗中立可能遭受的外部压力。政府亦担心边疆部族动荡、伊拉克方向的不稳定与列强对油田和交通线的干预。
threats.en: Principal threats came from the Soviet Union in the north, British power in the south and at sea, and the danger that Iran’s neutrality could be violated amid widening war. The government also feared unrest among frontier tribes, instability from the Iraqi direction, and great-power interference in oil fields and communications.

description.zh: 职业化训练，拥有成熟的操典
description.en: Professional training with established manuals

description.zh: 士气一般，作战意志不稳定
description.en: Mediocre morale, inconsistent fighting will

description.zh: 喷火/零式/Bf109战斗机，争夺制空权
description.en: Spitfire/Zero/Bf109 fighter for air superiority

description.zh: 75-105mm榴弹炮，师级火力支援
description.en: 75-105mm howitzer, division-level fire support

description.zh: U型潜艇/美军舰队潜艇，破交战利器
description.en: U-boat/Fleet submarine for commerce raiding

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

populationDescription.zh: 1939年伊朗人口约1500万，大多数居住在农村，分布于高原农耕区、绿洲城市、里海湿润地带和部族迁徙地。人口增长缓慢，地区差异显著，国家卫生和教育体系仍在建设中。
populationDescription.en: Iran’s population in 1939 was about 15 million, with the great majority living in the countryside across plateau farming zones, oasis towns, the humid Caspian regions, and tribal migratory areas. Population growth was modest, regional disparities were large, and national health and education systems were still under construction.

description.zh: 伊朗首都，西亚政治中心
description.en: Iranian capital, West Asian political center

description.zh: 萨法维王朝首都，世界之半
description.en: Safavid capital, 'half the world'

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与官僚军人精英、城市大地主和商业上层、巴扎商人、中小地主、乌理玛、手工业者、城市工人、部族成员以及占人口多数的佃农和小农。国家现代化正在培育新的受教育中产阶层，但规模仍有限。
socialClasses.en: Major social classes included the royal-bureaucratic-military elite, urban great landlords and commercial upper strata, bazaar merchants, small and middling landholders, the ulama, artisans, urban workers, tribal populations, and the tenant and small peasant majority. Modernization was fostering a new educated middle class, but it remained limited in size.

allies.zh: 伊朗在1939年没有正式军事盟友，奉行谨慎中立；与德国保持重要经济和技术联系，同时试图与英国、苏联、土耳其、阿富汗和伊拉克维持可操作关系。
allies.en: Iran had no formal military allies in 1939 and pursued cautious neutrality; it maintained important economic and technical ties with Germany while trying to preserve workable relations with Britain, the Soviet Union, Turkey, Afghanistan, and Iraq.

enemies.zh: 无公开宣战敌国，但对英国和苏联长期抱有战略疑惧，担心两国借战争或安全名义干涉伊朗主权。
enemies.en: It had no openly declared wartime enemies, but viewed Britain and the Soviet Union with enduring strategic suspicion, fearing they might interfere with Iranian sovereignty under the pretext of war or security.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护独立、避免沦为英苏势力范围、吸引多元外国技术与贸易伙伴、并在大战初期保持中立为核心。礼萨·沙阿有意利用德国作为平衡英苏影响的第三方，但这种策略在地缘上十分脆弱。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving independence, avoiding absorption into British or Soviet spheres of influence, attracting diversified foreign technical and trading partners, and maintaining neutrality in the early war period. Reza Shah deliberately used Germany as a third-party counterweight to British and Soviet influence, but this strategy was geopolitically fragile.

era.zh: 世界大战时代

keyInnovations.zh: 跨伊朗铁路、现代公路网扩张、国家电报与邮政系统、初步航空力量、现代兵工厂、世俗高等教育机构、国家登记和集中行政体系。这些成就体现为制度与基础设施现代化，而非原创尖端科研突破。
keyInnovations.en: The Trans-Iranian Railway, expansion of modern road networks, national telegraph and postal systems, a nascent air arm, modern arsenals, secular higher education institutions, and centralized registration and administration. These achievements reflected institutional and infrastructural modernization rather than original cutting-edge scientific breakthroughs.

strengths.zh: 国家权力在礼萨·沙阿统治下显著集中，军队、官僚体系和交通建设增强了中央控制；石油提供了关键外汇来源；民族主义动员和制度改革提高了国家整合能力。
strengths.en: State power had become markedly centralized under Reza Shah; the army, bureaucracy, and transport construction strengthened central control; oil provided a crucial source of foreign exchange; and nationalist mobilization and institutional reform improved national integration.

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础仍薄弱，农村贫困广泛，工业化有限，财政对关税和石油收入依赖较高；政权压制宗教与部族势力虽短期有效，却积累社会不满；面对英苏等大国时军事防御能力不足。
weaknesses.en: The economic base remained weak, rural poverty was widespread, industrialization was limited, and public finance depended heavily on customs and oil revenue; the regime’s suppression of clerical and tribal forces, though effective in the short term, accumulated social resentment; and military defenses were inadequate against powers such as Britain and the Soviet Union.

outlook.zh: 1939年的巴列维伊朗表面稳定，现代化成果可见，但其安全环境极其危险。若欧洲战争扩大并波及中东，伊朗的中立将面临严峻考验，特别是在德国联系、石油资源和战略交通线问题上，未来数年存在主权受侵害的高风险。
outlook.en: Pahlavi Iran in 1939 appeared stable and visibly modernizing, but its security environment was extremely dangerous. If the European war widened into the Middle East, Iranian neutrality would face severe strain; because of German ties, oil resources, and strategic communications, the next few years carried a high risk of violation of sovereignty.

description.zh: 伊朗在礼萨·沙阿统治下推进现代化，并试图保持中立。
description.en: Under Reza Shah, Iran pursued modernization while attempting to remain neutral.

governmentForm.zh: 名义上的君主立宪制王国，在强大王权、议会政治与英国影响之间运作
governmentForm.en: A nominal constitutional monarchy operating between strong royal authority, parliamentary politics, and substantial British influence

socialStructure.zh: 社会以逊尼派阿拉伯政治精英为核心，上层包括王室、地主、部族酋长、军官与城市显贵；什叶派阿拉伯农民构成南部人口主体，库尔德人主要分布于北部，另有亚述人、土库曼人、犹太人、亚美尼亚人及其他少数社群。城乡差距、部族关系与宗派差异对政治和行政具有持续影响。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on a Sunni Arab political elite, with the upper strata composed of the royal family, large landowners, tribal shaykhs, army officers, and urban notables. Shi'a Arab peasants formed much of the southern population, while Kurds predominated in the north; Assyrians, Turkmens, Jews, Armenians, and other minorities were also present. Urban-rural inequality, tribal ties, and sectarian differences continued to shape politics and administration.

rulingClass.zh: 哈希姆王室、逊尼派官僚与军官集团、议会中的地主与部族领袖、巴格达与摩苏尔等城市商人精英
rulingClass.en: The Hashemite royal house, Sunni bureaucratic and military elites, landed and tribal leaders in parliament, and urban commercial notables in cities such as Baghdad and Mosul

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，按哈希姆王朝父系继承；1939年4月加齐一世死后，由未成年的费萨尔二世继位并由摄政王阿卜杜勒·伊拉赫辅政
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy by patrilineal succession within the Hashemite dynasty; after Ghazi I died in April 1939, the underage Faisal II succeeded, with 'Abd al-Ilah serving as regent

structure.zh: 中央政府由国王、首相与内阁、两院制议会及各部组成。尽管宪法赋予议会职能，实际政治常受宫廷、军队、高级官僚、地方显贵与英国顾问共同影响。
structure.en: The central government consisted of the king, prime minister and cabinet, a bicameral parliament, and ministerial departments. Although the constitution granted parliamentary functions, real politics was often shaped jointly by the court, army, senior bureaucracy, provincial notables, and British advisers.

function.zh: 负责地方行政、治安、警察、户籍、部族自治事务与省级监督
function.en: Oversaw local administration, internal security, police, civil registration, tribal affairs, and provincial supervision

function.zh: 负责预算、税收、国债、海关收入分配、审计与国家金库
function.en: Managed the budget, taxation, public debt, customs revenue allocation, auditing, and the state treasury

function.zh: 负责陆军建设、军官任命、军需、训练与国防规划
function.en: Handled army organization, officer appointments, procurement, training, and defense planning

function.zh: 负责对英关系、国际条约、驻外使团与区域外交
function.en: Managed relations with Britain, international treaties, foreign missions, and regional diplomacy

function.zh: 管理法院、检察、监狱、法典实施与司法行政
function.en: Administered courts, prosecution, prisons, legal codes, and judicial administration

function.zh: 负责公立学校、师范教育、课程设置与有限的高等教育发展
function.en: Oversaw public schools, teacher training, curricula, and limited higher educational development

function.zh: 负责公共工程、道路、铁路、邮电、农业推广与部分商业监管
function.en: Supervised public works, roads, railways, posts and telegraphs, agricultural extension, and some commercial regulation

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为省级行政单位，由中央任命的省长和地方官管理；在广大乡村和荒漠地带，政府需依赖部族酋长、地方地主及治安力量贯彻命令。北部库尔德地区与南部部族地区的实际控制力不均。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provinces administered by centrally appointed governors and local officials; across much of the countryside and desert, the state relied on tribal shaykhs, landowners, and security forces to enforce its directives. Effective control varied, especially in the Kurdish north and tribal south.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为奥斯曼遗产、欧陆成文法、英国影响与伊斯兰法并存。民商法和刑法逐步现代化，但属人法尤其婚姻继承领域仍多受宗教法庭管辖。部族地区在实践中常结合国家法与习惯法。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Ottoman inheritances, continental-style codified law, British influence, and Islamic law. Civil, commercial, and criminal law had been increasingly modernized, but personal status matters, especially marriage and inheritance, remained heavily under religious courts. In tribal regions, state law often operated alongside customary practice.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要依赖石油特许与相关收益、关税、消费税、土地税和各类行政收费。直接税征收能力有限，农村征税受地主、部族关系和行政渗透不足制约。
taxationSystem.en: State revenues depended chiefly on oil concession-related income, customs duties, excise taxes, land taxes, and administrative fees. Capacity for direct taxation was limited, and rural collection was constrained by landlord power, tribal relations, and uneven administrative penetration.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教为主，人口多数为什叶派穆斯林，统治精英与许多城市官僚以逊尼派为主；并有基督徒、犹太教徒、雅兹迪派及其他宗教少数群体。宗教在社会生活和身份认同中具有重要地位。
religion.en: Islam predominated, with a Shi'a Muslim majority in the population, while the ruling elite and many urban officials were mainly Sunni; Christians, Jews, Yazidis, and other religious minorities were also present. Religion played a major role in social life and identity.

philosophy.zh: 国家意识形态混合阿拉伯民族主义、君主保守主义、有限宪政观念与现代化追求。教育界和军界中流行泛阿拉伯主义，同时传统部族价值、伊斯兰伦理与地方认同仍然强劲。
philosophy.en: State ideology mixed Arab nationalism, monarchical conservatism, limited constitutionalism, and aspirations toward modernization. Pan-Arab ideas circulated strongly in educational and military circles, while traditional tribal values, Islamic ethics, and local identities remained powerful.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语；库尔德语在部分地区使用阿拉伯字母或其他书写形式，行政与教育的官方主导文字为阿拉伯文。
writingSystem.en: Arabic was written chiefly in the Arabic script; Kurdish in some areas used Arabic-based or other writing forms, but Arabic was the principal official language of administration and education.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1930年代伊拉克在广播、报刊、学校扩张、现代诗歌、历史写作与城市文化生活方面取得进展。巴格达成为王国政治与文化中心，国家仪式、军乐、报业和知识分子圈强化了新兴伊拉克民族认同。
culturalAchievements.en: In the 1930s Iraq advanced in radio, newspapers, school expansion, modern poetry, historical writing, and urban cultural life. Baghdad emerged as the kingdom's political and cultural center, and state ceremony, military music, the press, and intellectual circles helped strengthen a developing Iraqi national identity.

languageFamily.zh: 以闪米特语系的阿拉伯语为官方主导语言，另有印欧语系伊朗语族的库尔德语，以及亚拉姆语、土耳其语等少数语言
languageFamily.en: Arabic of the Semitic family was the dominant official language, alongside Kurdish of the Iranian branch of Indo-European, as well as Neo-Aramaic, Turkish, and other minority languages

gdpDescription.zh: 1939年的伊拉克经济仍以农业和地租结构为基础，但石油部门的重要性迅速上升。总体经济规模在中东地区已具一定分量，但工业化程度低，城乡分化明显，交通与灌溉等基础设施仍在建设中。
