rulingClass.en: The British governor and colonial administrators, European plantation owners, major Indian traders, and African chiefs collaborating with the colonial authorities

succession.zh: 总督由英国殖民部任命，不存在本地继承机制。非洲酋长依部族惯例继位，但须获殖民当局承认。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British Colonial Office; there was no local succession mechanism. African chiefs succeeded by tribal custom but required colonial approval.

structure.zh: 1939年坦噶尼喀作为英国委任统治地，由总督主导行政，行政委员会为顾问机构，立法委员会以官方成员为主。间接统治制度下非洲酋长负责基层治理。
structure.en: In 1939 Tanganyika, as a British mandate, was administered by the governor, with an Executive Council as advisory body and a Legislative Council dominated by official members. Under indirect rule, African chiefs handled grassroots governance.

function.zh: 处理行政命令、政策协调和与伦敦殖民部的联络。
function.en: Handled administrative directives, policy coordination, and liaison with the Colonial Office in London.

function.zh: 管理间接统治制度、酋长任命和非洲人社区事务。
function.en: Managed the indirect rule system, appointment of chiefs, and African community affairs.

function.zh: 管理种植园许可、农业研究、土地分配和经济作物推广。
function.en: Managed plantation licensing, agricultural research, land allocation, and cash crop promotion.

localAdmin.zh: 全境分为若干省和区，由英国行政官管理。在间接统治框架下，非洲酋长的本地法庭和税收体系发挥基层治理功能。
localAdmin.en: The territory was divided into provinces and districts administered by British officials. Under indirect rule, African chiefs' local courts and tax systems served grassroots governance functions.

legalSystem.zh: 英国殖民法律为主，辅以非洲习惯法（在非洲人社区适用），伊斯兰法在沿海穆斯林社区部分适用。
legalSystem.en: British colonial law was primary, supplemented by African customary law (applied in African communities) and Islamic law partially applied in coastal Muslim communities.

taxationSystem.zh: 以人头税、出口关税和种植园许可费为主要收入来源。非洲农民须缴纳人头税以强制进入现金经济。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came primarily from poll taxes, export duties, and plantation license fees. African farmers were required to pay a poll tax to force entry into the cash economy.

religion.zh: 原住民传统信仰、基督教（传教活动持续扩展）和伊斯兰教（沿海地区为主）并存
religion.en: Indigenous traditional religions, Christianity (missionary activities expanding), and Islam (mainly coastal) coexisted

culturalAchievements.zh: 斯瓦希里语作为通用语言促进了区域文化整合。沿海的斯瓦希里城市文化、内陆的马赛等部族文化传统丰富多样。
culturalAchievements.en: Swahili as a lingua franca facilitated regional cultural integration. Coastal Swahili urban culture and inland traditions of groups such as the Maasai were richly diverse.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言为英语；通用语为斯瓦希里语（尼日尔-刚果语系班图语支）；另有数十种班图语和尼罗-撒哈拉语
languageFamily.en: Official language was English; the lingua franca was Swahili (Niger-Congo, Bantu); dozens of other Bantu and Nilo-Saharan languages were also spoken

philosophy.zh: 社会价值观以部族传统、祖先崇拜和社区互助为核心，同时受到基督教传教和伊斯兰文化的影响。
philosophy.en: Social values centered on tribal tradition, ancestor veneration, and community mutual aid, while also influenced by Christian missions and Islamic culture.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母（英文和斯瓦希里文）；沿海地区也使用阿拉伯字母书写斯瓦希里文。
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet (for English and Swahili); the Arabic script was also used for Swahili in coastal areas.

gdpDescription.zh: 以自给农业和经济作物出口为基础的殖民经济，主要出口剑麻、咖啡、棉花和丁香。基础设施集中于出口走廊，内陆广大地区经济自给。
gdpDescription.en: A colonial economy based on subsistence agriculture and cash crop exports, mainly sisal, coffee, cotton, and cloves. Infrastructure was concentrated along export corridors, with the vast interior economically self-sufficient.

mainIndustries.zh: 剑麻种植与加工、咖啡种植、棉花种植、丁香生产、矿业（金、锡）、自给农牧业
mainIndustries.en: Sisal cultivation and processing, coffee growing, cotton cultivation, clove production, mining (gold, tin), subsistence farming and herding

tradeGoods.zh: 剑麻、咖啡、棉花、丁香、黄金、兽皮；进口纺织品、金属制品、机械和石油
tradeGoods.en: Sisal, coffee, cotton, cloves, gold, hides; imports of textiles, metalware, machinery, and petroleum

householdWealth.zh: 欧洲种植园主和印度裔商人拥有最多货币财富，非洲人家庭财富主要表现为牲畜、土地使用权和农产品。
householdWealth.en: European planters and Indian traders held the most monetary wealth; African household wealth was mainly in livestock, land-use rights, and crops.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民出口导向型经济，欧洲种植园与非洲自给农业并存，印度裔商人控制零售和中间贸易。
economicSystem.en: A colonial export-oriented economy with European plantations coexisting alongside African subsistence farming, and Indian traders controlling retail and intermediate trade.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地财政基本自给，剑麻和咖啡出口税为主要收入来源，偶有伦敦拨款补助。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial finances were largely self-sustaining, with sisal and coffee export duties as the main revenue source, occasionally supplemented by grants from London.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以殖民地财政自给为原则，优先保障行政运转和出口基础设施，社会服务投入有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Based on the principle of colonial financial self-sufficiency, prioritizing administrative operations and export infrastructure, with limited investment in social services.

name.zh: 国王非洲步枪团（坦噶尼喀营）

description.zh: 以非洲士兵为主、英国军官指挥的殖民地军队，负责内部安全和边防任务。
description.en: A colonial force of mainly African soldiers under British officers, responsible for internal security and border duties.

description.zh: 负责城镇治安和刑事执法。
description.en: Responsible for urban security and criminal law enforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督马克·杨爵士（名义上），实际军事指挥由英国驻东非军事司令部负责
commanderInChief.en: Governor Sir Mark Young (nominally), with effective military command under British East Africa Command

technology.zh: 标准殖民地步兵装备：步枪、轻机枪和基本通讯设备。无装甲车辆或军用飞机。
technology.en: Standard colonial infantry equipment: rifles, light machine guns, and basic communications. No armored vehicles or military aircraft.

threats.zh: 1939年主要安全关切为意属东非（厄立特里亚、索马里兰、埃塞俄比亚）可能的入侵威胁。地方骚乱和部族冲突为次要威胁。
threats.en: In 1939 the main security concern was the potential invasion threat from Italian East Africa (Eritrea, Somaliland, Ethiopia). Local unrest and tribal conflicts were secondary threats.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

populationDescription.zh: 人口约530万，以班图语系非洲人为主体，另有约2.5万印度裔、9,000欧洲人和少量阿拉伯裔居民。主要族群包括苏库马、查加、哈亚等。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 5.3 million, predominantly Bantu-speaking Africans, with about 25,000 Indians, 9,000 Europeans, and a small Arab community. Major ethnic groups included the Sukuma, Chagga, and Haya.

socialClasses.zh: 欧洲行政官和种植园主居顶层，印度裔商人为中间层，非洲酋长和教育精英为过渡层，广大非洲农牧民为底层。种族等级制度明显。
socialClasses.en: European administrators and planters at the top, Indian traders as an intermediate layer, African chiefs and educated elites as a transitional layer, and the broad mass of African farmers and herders at the base. Racial hierarchy was pronounced.

allies.zh: 作为英国委任统治地，外交完全由伦敦处理。与邻近的肯尼亚和乌干达同属英属东非体系。
allies.en: As a British mandate, foreign affairs were entirely handled by London. Tanganyika belonged to the British East African system alongside neighboring Kenya and Uganda.

enemies.zh: 意属东非为主要潜在敌方——意大利对东非的扩张野心是最大外部威胁。
enemies.en: Italian East Africa was the main potential adversary — Italy's expansionist ambitions in East Africa were the greatest external threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无独立外交，完全服从英国帝国政策。作为委任统治地，名义上接受国际联盟监督。
foreignPolicy.en: No independent foreign policy; entirely subordinate to British imperial policy. As a mandate territory, nominally subject to League of Nations oversight.

era.zh: 殖民地初期现代化阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 铁路线（中央线和坦噶线）、达累斯萨拉姆港口设施、剑麻加工机械、基础公共卫生措施和有限电力供应。
keyInnovations.en: Railway lines (Central and Tanga lines), Dar es Salaam port facilities, sisal processing machinery, basic public health measures, and limited electricity supply.

strengths.zh: 丰富农业资源、战略东非海岸位置、英国殖民管理的相对秩序和间接统治制度减少了直接冲突。
strengths.en: Rich agricultural resources, strategic East African coastal location, relative order under British colonial administration, and indirect rule that reduced direct conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 经济严重依赖少数经济作物、基础设施极度不足、非洲人政治权利缺失、教育和医疗服务极度有限。
weaknesses.en: Heavy economic dependence on a few cash crops, severely inadequate infrastructure, lack of African political rights, and extremely limited education and health services.

outlook.zh: 二战期间坦噶尼喀将作为英军东非战场的重要后方基地，非洲士兵大量参战，战后民族意识将加速觉醒。
outlook.en: During WWII Tanganyika would serve as an important rear base for the British East African campaign; large numbers of African soldiers would serve, and post-war national consciousness would accelerate.

description.zh: 1939年的坦噶尼喀是英国委任统治下的东非殖民地，以剑麻和咖啡出口为经济支柱，面临意属东非的潜在军事威胁。
description.en: Tanganyika in 1939 was an East African territory under British mandate rule, with sisal and coffee exports as economic pillars, facing a potential military threat from Italian East Africa.

name.zh: 肯尼亚殖民地与保护领

ruler.zh: 罗伯特·布鲁克-波帕姆爵士

governmentForm.zh: 英国直辖殖民地，由总督和行政委员会管辖，立法委员会中欧洲定居者、印度裔和阿拉伯裔各有代表，非洲人仅有象征性代表。
governmentForm.en: A British Crown Colony governed by a governor and executive council, with a legislative council representing European settlers, Indians, and Arabs, while Africans had only token representation.

socialStructure.zh: 社会按种族分层：约3万欧洲定居者占据高地最佳农业区，约4.5万印度裔从事商贸和铁路工作，约330万非洲人多为农牧民或劳工。白人高地农场制度是种族不平等的核心。
socialStructure.en: Society was stratified by race: about 30,000 European settlers occupied the best highland farmland, about 45,000 Indians engaged in commerce and railway work, and about 3.3 million Africans were mostly farmers, herders, or laborers. The White Highlands farm system was central to racial inequality.

rulingClass.zh: 英国总督和殖民官员、欧洲大农场主（尤其是高地咖啡和茶叶种植者）、印度裔商业巨头
rulingClass.en: The British governor and colonial officials, European large-scale farmers (especially highland coffee and tea planters), and Indian commercial magnates

succession.zh: 总督由英国殖民部任命。非洲酋长依部族惯例继位但须获殖民当局认可。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British Colonial Office. African chiefs succeeded by tribal custom but required colonial approval.

structure.zh: 1939年肯尼亚由总督主导行政，立法委员会代表多元种族群体但权力不均。白人定居者在立法委员会中影响力最大。区行政官和省专员构成地方治理框架。
structure.en: In 1939 Kenya was administered by the governor; the Legislative Council represented multiple racial groups but unequally. White settlers wielded the most influence in the council. District officers and provincial commissioners formed the local governance framework.

function.zh: 协调殖民行政、政策制定和与伦敦的联络。
function.en: Coordinated colonial administration, policy-making, and liaison with London.

name.en: Department of Agriculture and Veterinary Services

function.zh: 管理白人高地农业、兽医服务和非洲人保留地农业推广。
function.en: Managed White Highland agriculture, veterinary services, and African reserve agricultural extension.

function.zh: 管理非洲人保留地、劳工招募、酋长事务和基层司法。
function.en: Managed African reserves, labor recruitment, chieftaincy affairs, and grassroots justice.

localAdmin.zh: 全殖民地分为省和区，由省专员和区行政官管理。非洲人保留地由政府指定的酋长和长老会负责日常事务。
localAdmin.en: The colony was divided into provinces and districts administered by provincial commissioners and district officers. African reserves were managed by government-designated chiefs and councils of elders.

legalSystem.zh: 英国殖民法为主，辅以非洲习惯法和伊斯兰法（沿海地区）。种族歧视性立法广泛存在，如通行证制度和土地隔离法规。
legalSystem.en: British colonial law was primary, supplemented by African customary law and Islamic law (in coastal areas). Racially discriminatory legislation was widespread, including pass laws and land segregation regulations.

taxationSystem.zh: 以关税、人头税（非洲人）、所得税（欧洲人和印度裔）和出口税为主要收入。人头税迫使非洲人进入工资劳动市场。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from customs duties, poll taxes (on Africans), income taxes (on Europeans and Indians), and export duties. The poll tax forced Africans into the wage labor market.

religion.zh: 非洲传统信仰为主，基督教传教迅速扩展（尤其在基库尤人中），沿海地区伊斯兰教传统深厚
religion.en: African traditional religions predominated, with Christianity spreading rapidly through missions (especially among the Kikuyu), and Islam deeply rooted in coastal areas

culturalAchievements.zh: 丰富多元的东非文化传统——基库尤、卢欧、坎巴等族群各有独特的口述文学、音乐和工艺。蒙巴萨的斯瓦希里文化和拉穆古城具有重要文化价值。
culturalAchievements.en: Rich and diverse East African cultural traditions — the Kikuyu, Luo, Kamba, and other groups each had distinctive oral literature, music, and crafts. Mombasa's Swahili culture and Lamu Old Town held important cultural value.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言为英语；主要非洲语言包括基库尤语、卢欧语、坎巴语（班图和尼罗-撒哈拉语系）；斯瓦希里语为通用语；沿海通用阿拉伯语
languageFamily.en: Official language was English; major African languages included Kikuyu, Luo, Kamba (Bantu and Nilo-Saharan families); Swahili served as lingua franca; Arabic was used on the coast

philosophy.zh: 传统部族价值观、基督教传教影响和殖民秩序并存。基库尤人中围绕女性割礼问题的文化-政治争论反映了传统与现代的张力。
philosophy.en: Traditional tribal values, Christian missionary influence, and the colonial order coexisted. Cultural-political debates among the Kikuyu over female circumcision reflected tensions between tradition and modernity.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母（英文、斯瓦希里文和主要非洲语言）；沿海也使用阿拉伯字母
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet (for English, Swahili, and major African languages); Arabic script also used on the coast

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以白人高地农业（咖啡、茶叶、剑麻、除虫菊）为出口支柱，蒙巴萨港为东非贸易枢纽。非洲人保留地经济以自给农牧为主。旅游（狩猎）也贡献收入。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was built on White Highland agriculture (coffee, tea, sisal, pyrethrum) as the export pillar, with Mombasa port as the East African trade hub. African reserve economies were mainly subsistence. Tourism (safari hunting) also contributed revenue.

mainIndustries.zh: 咖啡和茶叶种植、剑麻种植、除虫菊生产、畜牧业、蒙巴萨港贸易、狩猎旅游
mainIndustries.en: Coffee and tea cultivation, sisal growing, pyrethrum production, livestock, Mombasa port trade, safari tourism

tradeGoods.zh: 咖啡、茶叶、剑麻、除虫菊、兽皮、碳酸钠；进口机械、纺织品、石油和消费品
tradeGoods.en: Coffee, tea, sisal, pyrethrum, hides, soda ash; imports of machinery, textiles, petroleum, and consumer goods

householdWealth.zh: 欧洲农场主拥有大面积高地农场和出口收入，印度裔商人控制零售业，非洲人家庭以牲畜、土地使用权和少量农产品为主要财富。
householdWealth.en: European farmers owned large highland farms and export income; Indian traders controlled retail; African household wealth was mainly in livestock, land-use rights, and small agricultural output.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民种植园经济，白人高地体制为核心，非洲人被限制在保留地或充当低薪劳工，印度裔充当商业中间层。
economicSystem.en: A colonial plantation economy centered on the White Highlands system, with Africans confined to reserves or serving as low-wage labor, and Indians as a commercial intermediary layer.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地财政自给能力较强，咖啡和茶叶出口税为主要收入支柱。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial finances were relatively self-sustaining, with coffee and tea export duties as the main revenue pillars.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政优先服务白人定居者经济利益和殖民行政开支，对非洲人教育和社会服务投入偏低。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized white settler economic interests and colonial administrative spending, with low investment in African education and social services.

name.zh: 国王非洲步枪团（肯尼亚营）

description.zh: 以非洲士兵为主、英国军官指挥的殖民地步兵，为东非英军最重要的作战力量之一。
description.en: Colonial infantry of mainly African soldiers under British officers, among the most important fighting forces of the British military in East Africa.

name.zh: 肯尼亚防卫军（白人志愿军）

description.zh: 白人定居者组成的志愿兵力量，1939年正在动员扩编。
description.en: A volunteer force of white settlers, being mobilized and expanded in 1939.

description.zh: 负责城镇和农村治安。
description.en: Responsible for urban and rural security.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督（名义上），实际由英国东非司令部指挥
commanderInChief.en: The Governor (nominally), with effective command under British East Africa Command

technology.zh: 标准殖民地步兵装备：步枪、轻机枪和基本通讯设备。1939年起开始接收更多英国军事物资。
technology.en: Standard colonial infantry equipment: rifles, light machine guns, and basic communications. From 1939 began receiving more British military supplies.

threats.zh: 1939年最大威胁来自意属东非（埃塞俄比亚和索马里兰），意大利在非洲之角的军事部署对肯尼亚北部构成直接威胁。
threats.en: In 1939 the greatest threat came from Italian East Africa (Ethiopia and Somaliland); Italy's military deployments in the Horn of Africa posed a direct threat to northern Kenya.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

populationDescription.zh: 人口约350万，以基库尤、卢欧、坎巴、卡伦金等班图和尼罗语系非洲族群为主体，约3万欧洲定居者、4.5万印度裔和少量阿拉伯裔。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 3.5 million, mainly Bantu and Nilotic African groups including Kikuyu, Luo, Kamba, and Kalenjin, with about 30,000 European settlers, 45,000 Indians, and a small Arab community.

description.zh: 东非科技与金融中心
description.en: East African tech and financial hub

socialClasses.zh: 欧洲高地农场主和殖民官员居顶层，印度裔商人为中间层，受过教育的非洲精英为过渡层，广大非洲农牧民为底层。
socialClasses.en: European highland farmers and colonial officials at the top, Indian traders as an intermediate layer, educated African elites as a transitional stratum, and the broad mass of African farmers and herders at the base.

allies.zh: 作为英国殖民地，外交由伦敦处理。与乌干达和坦噶尼喀同属英属东非体系，共享铁路和关税联盟。
allies.en: As a British colony, foreign affairs were handled by London. Shared the British East African system with Uganda and Tanganyika, including a common railway and customs union.

enemies.zh: 意属东非为最大威胁——1940-1941年意大利将从埃塞俄比亚和索马里兰方向进攻肯尼亚。
enemies.en: Italian East Africa was the greatest threat — in 1940-1941 Italy would attack Kenya from Ethiopia and Somaliland.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无独立外交，完全服从英国帝国政策。作为东非英国殖民体系的核心，肯尼亚在帝国战略中具有重要地位。
foreignPolicy.en: No independent foreign policy; entirely subordinate to British imperial policy. As the core of the British colonial system in East Africa, Kenya held an important position in imperial strategy.

era.zh: 殖民地初步现代化阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 乌干达铁路（蒙巴萨至基苏木）、内罗毕城市建设、咖啡加工设施、基础医疗和教育设施、无线电通讯。
keyInnovations.en: The Uganda Railway (Mombasa to Kisumu), Nairobi urban development, coffee processing facilities, basic medical and educational facilities, and radio communications.

strengths.zh: 蒙巴萨港为东非贸易枢纽、高地农业具有竞争力、地理多样性支撑多元经济潜力、英国帝国防御体系提供安全保障。
strengths.en: Mombasa port as the East African trade hub, competitive highland agriculture, geographic diversity supporting economic potential, and the British imperial defense system providing security.

weaknesses.zh: 种族不平等尖锐（尤其是土地问题）、非洲人政治权利缺失、经济严重依赖少数经济作物、意属东非的军事威胁。
weaknesses.en: Acute racial inequality (especially in land ownership), lack of African political rights, heavy economic dependence on a few cash crops, and the military threat from Italian East Africa.

outlook.zh: 二战期间肯尼亚将成为英军东非战役的前线基地。大量非洲人参军参战将深刻改变社会意识，战后基库尤人的土地诉求将催生茅茅起义。
outlook.en: During WWII Kenya would become a front-line base for British East African campaigns. Mass African military service would profoundly change social consciousness, and post-war Kikuyu land grievances would give rise to the Mau Mau Uprising.

description.zh: 1939年的肯尼亚殖民地是英国东非殖民体系的核心，白人高地农场制度与多种族社会结构并存，面临来自意属东非的军事威胁。
description.en: Kenya Colony in 1939 was the core of the British East African colonial system, with the White Highlands farm system and a multiracial social structure, facing a military threat from Italian East Africa.

governmentForm.zh: 英国保护领，由总督管辖。布干达王国等本地王国保留一定自治权，形成间接统治与传统王权并存的独特格局。
governmentForm.en: A British protectorate governed by a governor. Local kingdoms such as Buganda retained some autonomy, creating a unique arrangement of indirect rule coexisting with traditional kingship.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以布干达等传统王国为核心组织框架。非洲人口占绝大多数，印度裔商人（约1.5万）控制商贸，欧洲人极少（约2,000人）主要为殖民官员和传教士。布干达贵族在间接统治下保持较大权力。
socialStructure.en: Society was organized around traditional kingdoms such as Buganda. Africans were the overwhelming majority; Indian traders (~15,000) controlled commerce, while Europeans (~2,000) were mainly colonial officials and missionaries. The Buganda aristocracy retained considerable power under indirect rule.

rulingClass.zh: 英国总督和殖民官员、布干达国王（卡巴卡）和贵族、印度裔大商人
rulingClass.en: The British governor and colonial officials, the Kabaka (king) and nobility of Buganda, and major Indian traders

succession.zh: 总督由英国殖民部任命。布干达卡巴卡和其他传统王国统治者依本地王族继承规则传位，但须获殖民当局认可。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British Colonial Office. The Buganda Kabaka and other traditional kingdom rulers succeeded by local royal succession rules, but required colonial approval.

structure.zh: 1939年乌干达由英国总督主导行政，布干达王国通过1900年《乌干达协议》享有较大内部自治。其他王国（布尼奥罗、托罗、安科莱）也有不同程度的传统自治。总督下设行政委员会和立法委员会。
structure.en: In 1939 Uganda was administered by the British governor. The Buganda Kingdom enjoyed considerable internal autonomy through the 1900 Uganda Agreement. Other kingdoms (Bunyoro, Toro, Ankole) also had varying degrees of traditional autonomy. Executive and legislative councils operated under the governor.

function.zh: 协调殖民行政和与伦敦的联络。
function.en: Coordinated colonial administration and liaison with London.

function.zh: 管理棉花和咖啡种植推广、农业研究和出口质量控制。
function.en: Managed cotton and coffee cultivation promotion, agricultural research, and export quality control.

name.zh: 原住民事务与地方政府局
name.en: Department of Native Affairs and Local Government

function.zh: 管理与传统王国的关系、间接统治和地方政府事务。
function.en: Managed relations with traditional kingdoms, indirect rule, and local government affairs.

localAdmin.zh: 布干达享有最大自治权，由卢基科（议会）和首相（卡提基罗）管理日常事务。其他王国和非王国地区由殖民地区行政官和传统领袖共同治理。
localAdmin.en: Buganda enjoyed the greatest autonomy, with the Lukiiko (parliament) and Katikiro (prime minister) managing daily affairs. Other kingdoms and non-kingdom areas were jointly governed by colonial district officers and traditional leaders.

legalSystem.zh: 英国殖民法、布干达传统法律和非洲习惯法并行。布干达法院在特定民事案件中保持管辖权。
legalSystem.en: British colonial law, Buganda traditional law, and African customary law operated in parallel. Buganda courts retained jurisdiction in certain civil cases.

taxationSystem.zh: 以棉花出口税、人头税、关税和许可费为主要收入。布干达政府也征收本地税收维持自治运作。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from cotton export taxes, poll taxes, customs duties, and license fees. The Buganda government also collected local taxes to fund autonomous operations.

religion.zh: 基督教（天主教和新教各占重要地位，因历史上的宗教战争而分化）、伊斯兰教和非洲传统信仰并存
religion.en: Christianity (both Catholic and Protestant held important positions, divided by historical religious wars), Islam, and African traditional religions coexisted

culturalAchievements.zh: 布干达王国拥有丰富的宫廷文化传统，包括精美的树皮布工艺（后被列为UNESCO非遗）、口述史诗和宫廷音乐。各族群均保有独特的文化传统。
culturalAchievements.en: The Buganda Kingdom had a rich court culture tradition, including exquisite bark cloth craftsmanship (later listed as UNESCO intangible heritage), oral epics, and court music. Each ethnic group maintained distinct cultural traditions.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言为英语；主要非洲语言为卢干达语（班图语支），另有阿乔利语、兰戈语（尼罗语系）等
languageFamily.en: Official language was English; the main African language was Luganda (Bantu); others included Acholi, Lango (Nilotic), and more

philosophy.zh: 传统王国政治文化与基督教传教文明并存。布干达的宫廷政治传统和贵族意识深厚，北方尼罗语系族群则保持更为平等的部族组织。
philosophy.en: Traditional kingdom political culture coexisted with Christian missionary civilization. Buganda's court political tradition and aristocratic consciousness were deep, while northern Nilotic groups maintained more egalitarian tribal organization.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母（英文和非洲语言均使用拉丁字母书写）
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet (used for both English and African languages)

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以非洲小农种植的棉花和咖啡出口为主（与肯尼亚不同，乌干达无白人大农场），印度裔商人控制棉花收购和零售网络。基础设施以出口铁路走廊为主。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on cotton and coffee exports grown by African smallholders (unlike Kenya, Uganda had no white large-scale farms); Indian traders controlled cotton buying and retail networks. Infrastructure centered on the export railway corridor.

mainIndustries.zh: 棉花种植与轧棉、咖啡种植、少量糖业、自给农牧业
mainIndustries.en: Cotton cultivation and ginning, coffee growing, small-scale sugar production, subsistence farming and herding

tradeGoods.zh: 棉花（主导出口品）、咖啡、兽皮、糖；进口纺织品、金属制品、机械和消费品
tradeGoods.en: Cotton (dominant export), coffee, hides, sugar; imports of textiles, metalware, machinery, and consumer goods

householdWealth.zh: 布干达的非洲地主和酋长拥有较多土地和现金收入。印度裔商人积累了商业财富。普通非洲农民以土地、牲畜和农产品为主要财富。
householdWealth.en: Buganda's African landowners and chiefs held more land and cash income. Indian traders accumulated commercial wealth. Ordinary African farmers' wealth was mainly in land, livestock, and crops.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民小农出口经济，非洲农民种植棉花和咖啡，印度裔商人充当收购和零售中间人，最终产品出口至英国市场。
economicSystem.en: A colonial smallholder export economy where African farmers grew cotton and coffee, Indian traders served as buying and retail intermediaries, and final products were exported to the British market.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地财政基本自给，棉花出口税为主要收入来源。布干达政府另有独立的财政收支。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial finances were largely self-sustaining, with cotton export tax as the main revenue source. The Buganda government also had its own independent fiscal operations.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以殖民地财政自给为原则，棉花出口税为核心收入，社会服务支出相对有限但高于一些邻近殖民地。
fiscalPolicy.en: Based on colonial financial self-sufficiency, with cotton export tax as core revenue; social service spending was relatively limited but higher than in some neighboring colonies.

name.zh: 国王非洲步枪团（乌干达营）

description.zh: 以非洲士兵为主、英国军官指挥的殖民地步兵。乌干达营主要从北方尼罗语系族群招募。
description.en: Colonial infantry of mainly African soldiers under British officers. Uganda battalions recruited mainly from northern Nilotic groups.

description.zh: 负责城镇治安和一般执法。
description.en: Responsible for urban security and general law enforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督（名义上），实际由英国东非司令部指挥
commanderInChief.en: The Governor (nominally), with effective command under British East Africa Command

technology.zh: 标准殖民地步兵装备：步枪和轻机枪。无装甲车辆或空军。
technology.en: Standard colonial infantry equipment: rifles and light machine guns. No armored vehicles or air force.

threats.zh: 与肯尼亚类似，意属东非是主要外部威胁。内部安全主要关切为地方骚乱和布干达与其他族群的政治紧张。
threats.en: As with Kenya, Italian East Africa was the main external threat. Internal security concerns were mainly local unrest and political tensions between Buganda and other groups.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 75-105mm榴弹炮，师级火力支援
description.en: 75-105mm howitzer, division-level fire support

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

populationDescription.zh: 人口约380万，以班图语系（干达、索加等）和尼罗语系（阿乔利、兰戈等）非洲人为主体，约1.5万印度裔和约2,000欧洲人。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 3.8 million, mainly Bantu-speaking (Ganda, Soga, etc.) and Nilotic (Acholi, Lango, etc.) Africans, with about 15,000 Indians and about 2,000 Europeans.

socialClasses.zh: 殖民官员和欧洲传教士居顶层，印度裔商人为商业中间层，布干达贵族和受教育精英为非洲上层，普通非洲农民为社会基础。
socialClasses.en: Colonial officials and European missionaries at the top, Indian traders as a commercial middle layer, Buganda nobility and educated elites as the African upper stratum, and ordinary African farmers as the social base.

allies.zh: 作为英国保护领，外交由伦敦处理。与肯尼亚和坦噶尼喀同属英属东非体系。
allies.en: As a British protectorate, foreign affairs were handled by London. Part of the British East African system with Kenya and Tanganyika.

enemies.zh: 意属东非（尤其从埃塞俄比亚方向）为主要潜在威胁。
enemies.en: Italian East Africa (especially from the Ethiopian direction) was the main potential threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无独立外交，服从英国帝国政策。布干达王国试图维持其在殖民框架内的特殊地位。
foreignPolicy.en: No independent foreign policy; subordinate to British imperial policy. The Buganda Kingdom sought to maintain its special position within the colonial framework.

era.zh: 殖民地初期现代化阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 乌干达铁路延伸段、棉花轧棉厂、恩德培植物园与农业研究、基础教会学校和医院、金贾水力发电潜力初步勘探。
keyInnovations.en: Uganda Railway extension, cotton ginning factories, Entebbe Botanical Gardens and agricultural research, basic mission schools and hospitals, and initial surveying of Jinja's hydroelectric potential.

strengths.zh: 肥沃农业用地、传统王国的有效基层治理、棉花出口稳定收入、非洲人小农经济模式减少了种族冲突。
strengths.en: Fertile agricultural land, effective grassroots governance through traditional kingdoms, stable cotton export income, and an African smallholder model that reduced racial conflict.

weaknesses.zh: 经济极度依赖棉花单一出口、基础设施匮乏、教育覆盖率低、布干达与其他族群的政治不平衡。
weaknesses.en: Extreme economic dependence on cotton as a single export, inadequate infrastructure, low education coverage, and political imbalance between Buganda and other groups.

outlook.zh: 二战将刺激棉花需求，非洲士兵参战经验将促进政治觉醒，战后布干达的独特地位将成为独立运动中的复杂因素。
outlook.en: WWII would stimulate cotton demand; African soldiers' wartime experience would promote political awakening, and Buganda's unique status would become a complicating factor in the independence movement.

description.zh: 1939年的乌干达保护领是英国间接统治下的东非殖民地，布干达王国保持较大自治权，经济以非洲小农棉花出口为主。
description.en: Uganda Protectorate in 1939 was an East African colony under British indirect rule, where the Buganda Kingdom retained considerable autonomy and the economy was based on African smallholder cotton exports.

name.zh: 塞拉利昂殖民地与保护领

governmentForm.zh: 英国殖民地（弗里敦及半岛）与保护领（内陆）的双重体制。弗里敦由总督直接管辖，内陆通过间接统治由部落酋长治理。
governmentForm.en: A dual system of Crown Colony (Freetown and peninsula) and Protectorate (interior). Freetown was directly governed by the governor, while the interior was governed through indirect rule by tribal chiefs.

socialStructure.zh: 弗里敦社会以克里奥尔人（解放奴隶后裔）为受教育精英阶层，与内陆的曼丁戈、泰姆内、门德等原住民族群社会文化差异显著。殖民官员居于顶层，欧洲和黎巴嫩商人控制贸易。
socialStructure.en: Freetown society featured the Krio (descendants of freed slaves) as an educated elite, distinct from interior indigenous groups like Mende, Temne, and Mandinka. Colonial officials occupied the top tier, with European and Lebanese traders controlling commerce.

rulingClass.zh: 英国总督和殖民官员、克里奥尔精英（律师、医生、商人）、内陆部落酋长和黎巴嫩裔商人
rulingClass.en: The British governor and colonial officials, Krio elites (lawyers, doctors, merchants), interior tribal chiefs, and Lebanese traders

succession.zh: 总督由英国殖民部任命。内陆酋长依部族惯例继位，须获殖民当局承认。克里奥尔精英通过教育和专业成就获得社会地位。
succession.en: The governor was appointed by the British Colonial Office. Interior chiefs succeeded by tribal custom, requiring colonial approval. Krio elites achieved social status through education and professional attainment.

structure.zh: 1939年塞拉利昂由总督主导行政。殖民地（弗里敦）有较完善的英式市政治理，保护领通过区专员和酋长实行间接统治。立法委员会包含官方成员和少量非官方代表。
structure.en: In 1939 Sierra Leone was administered by the governor. The Colony (Freetown) had a relatively developed British-style municipal system, while the Protectorate was governed through district commissioners and chiefs under indirect rule. The Legislative Council included official members and a few unofficial representatives.

function.zh: 协调殖民行政和与伦敦的联络。
function.en: Coordinated colonial administration and liaison with London.

function.zh: 管理钻石和铁矿开采许可、地质勘探和矿业税收。
function.en: Managed diamond and iron mining licenses, geological surveys, and mining revenue.

function.zh: 管理间接统治、酋长事务和内陆地区行政。
function.en: Managed indirect rule, chieftaincy affairs, and interior administration.

localAdmin.zh: 弗里敦有市议会和较完善的市政管理。内陆保护领分为省和区，由区专员和部族酋长共同管辖。酋长法庭处理民事和部分刑事案件。
localAdmin.en: Freetown had a city council and relatively developed municipal administration. The interior Protectorate was divided into provinces and districts, jointly administered by district commissioners and tribal chiefs. Chiefs' courts handled civil and some criminal cases.

legalSystem.zh: 弗里敦适用英国普通法，保护领以非洲习惯法和殖民条例并行。伊斯兰法在穆斯林社区部分适用。
legalSystem.en: English common law applied in Freetown; the Protectorate used African customary law and colonial regulations in parallel. Islamic law was partially applied in Muslim communities.

taxationSystem.zh: 以进口关税、矿业特许权费、人头税（内陆）和港口费用为主要收入。弗里敦港的转口贸易贡献重要关税收入。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from import duties, mining royalties, poll taxes (in the interior), and port fees. Freetown port's entrepôt trade contributed important customs revenue.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教（尤其在北部和内陆曼丁戈等族群中）、基督教（弗里敦克里奥尔社区和传教地区）和非洲传统信仰并存
religion.en: Islam (especially among northern and interior Mandinka groups), Christianity (in the Freetown Krio community and missionary areas), and African traditional religions coexisted

culturalAchievements.zh: 弗里敦是西非最早的英语教育中心之一，福拉湾学院（1827年建立）是撒哈拉以南非洲第一所西式高等教育机构。克里奥尔文化融合了非洲、英国和加勒比元素。
culturalAchievements.en: Freetown was one of West Africa's earliest English-language education centers; Fourah Bay College (founded 1827) was the first Western-style institution of higher education in sub-Saharan Africa. Krio culture blended African, British, and Caribbean elements.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言为英语；克里奥尔语（英语克里奥尔）为弗里敦通用语；内陆主要语言为曼丁戈语、泰姆内语和门德语（尼日尔-刚果语系）
languageFamily.en: Official language was English; Krio (English-based creole) was Freetown's lingua franca; main interior languages were Mandinka, Temne, and Mende (Niger-Congo family)

philosophy.zh: 克里奥尔社区推崇英式教育和基督教价值观，内陆社会以伊斯兰学术传统和部族价值观为核心。波罗秘密社和桑德秘密社在社会组织中发挥重要作用。
philosophy.en: The Krio community valued British-style education and Christian values; interior society centered on Islamic scholarly tradition and tribal values. The Poro and Sande secret societies played important roles in social organization.

writingSystem.zh: 拉丁字母（英文和克里奥尔语）；阿拉伯字母在伊斯兰学术中使用；门德音节文字（基卡库伊）也有使用
writingSystem.en: Latin alphabet (for English and Krio); Arabic script used in Islamic scholarship; the Mende Kikakui syllabary was also in use

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以矿业（1930年发现钻石、铁矿）和农产品出口为主。弗里敦港是西非重要的转口贸易中心。大部分人口从事自给农业。黎巴嫩裔商人控制内陆零售网络。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was based on mining (diamonds discovered in 1930, iron ore) and agricultural exports. Freetown port was an important West African entrepôt. Most of the population engaged in subsistence farming. Lebanese traders controlled the interior retail network.

mainIndustries.zh: 钻石和铁矿开采、棕榈仁和棕榈油出口、可可种植、弗里敦港转口贸易、自给农业
mainIndustries.en: Diamond and iron ore mining, palm kernel and palm oil exports, cocoa cultivation, Freetown port entrepôt trade, subsistence agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 钻石、铁矿石、棕榈仁、棕榈油、可可；进口纺织品、食品、金属制品和机械
tradeGoods.en: Diamonds, iron ore, palm kernels, palm oil, cocoa; imports of textiles, food, metalware, and machinery

householdWealth.zh: 克里奥尔精英和黎巴嫩商人拥有较多财富和房产。内陆酋长控制地方资源。普通非洲人以土地和农产品为主要财富。
householdWealth.en: Krio elites and Lebanese merchants held more wealth and property. Interior chiefs controlled local resources. Ordinary Africans' wealth was mainly in land and crops.

economicSystem.zh: 殖民出口经济，矿业和农产品出口为支柱，黎巴嫩商人和欧洲公司控制主要商业网络。
economicSystem.en: A colonial export economy with mining and agricultural exports as pillars; Lebanese traders and European companies controlled the main commercial networks.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地财政规模有限但基本自给，钻石矿业税收正日益成为重要收入来源。
treasuryDescription.en: Colonial finances were limited in scale but largely self-sustaining; diamond mining revenue was becoming an increasingly important income source.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以殖民地财政自给为原则，依赖关税和矿业税收，社会服务支出有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Based on colonial financial self-sufficiency, relying on customs and mining revenue, with limited social service spending.

name.zh: 皇家西非前线部队（塞拉利昂营）
name.en: Royal West African Frontier Force (Sierra Leone battalion)

description.zh: 以非洲士兵为主、英国军官指挥的殖民地步兵，负责内部安全和帝国防御任务。
description.en: Colonial infantry of mainly African soldiers under British officers, responsible for internal security and imperial defense duties.

description.zh: 负责弗里敦和内陆主要城镇的治安。
description.en: Responsible for security in Freetown and main interior towns.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督（名义上），受英国西非司令部协调
commanderInChief.en: The Governor (nominally), coordinated with British West Africa Command

technology.zh: 标准殖民地轻步兵装备。弗里敦港有基础海防设施。
technology.en: Standard colonial light infantry equipment. Freetown port had basic naval defense facilities.

threats.zh: 外部军事威胁较低，但弗里敦港作为大西洋战略枢纽在战时具有重要价值，可能成为海上袭击目标。内部主要关切为1898年人头税战争后的地方稳定维持。
threats.en: External military threats were relatively low, but Freetown port's strategic value as an Atlantic hub made it a potential target in wartime. Internal concerns centered on maintaining stability since the 1898 Hut Tax War.

description.zh: 几乎无正规训练，依靠部落传承
description.en: Virtually untrained, relying on tribal traditions

description.zh: 士气崩溃，大规模逃亡
description.en: Morale collapsed, mass desertion

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

description.zh: 破片手榴弹，步兵必备
description.en: Fragmentation hand grenade, infantry essential

populationDescription.zh: 人口约180万，以门德、泰姆内、林巴等非洲族群为主体，弗里敦约3万克里奥尔人，另有少量欧洲人和黎巴嫩裔。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 1.8 million, predominantly Mende, Temne, Limba, and other African groups, with about 30,000 Krio in Freetown, and small European and Lebanese communities.

socialClasses.zh: 殖民官员和欧洲商人居顶层，克里奥尔精英和黎巴嫩商人为中间层，内陆酋长为地方权力持有者，普通非洲农民为底层。殖民地与保护领之间存在深刻社会文化鸿沟。
socialClasses.en: Colonial officials and European merchants at the top, Krio elites and Lebanese traders as an intermediate layer, interior chiefs as local power holders, and ordinary African farmers at the base. A deep socio-cultural divide existed between Colony and Protectorate.

allies.zh: 作为英国殖民地，外交由伦敦处理。弗里敦港在英国大西洋海军战略中具有重要地位。
allies.en: As a British colony, foreign affairs were handled by London. Freetown port held an important position in Britain's Atlantic naval strategy.

enemies.zh: 无直接敌国。战时弗里敦港将成为盟军大西洋护航集结点，可能面临德国潜艇威胁。
enemies.en: No direct enemies. In wartime Freetown port would become an Allied Atlantic convoy assembly point, potentially facing German submarine threats.

foreignPolicy.zh: 无独立外交，服从英国帝国政策。弗里敦港的大西洋战略位置在二战中将变得极为重要。
foreignPolicy.en: No independent foreign policy; subordinate to British imperial policy. Freetown port's strategic Atlantic position would become extremely important during WWII.

era.zh: 殖民地初期现代化阶段

keyInnovations.zh: 弗里敦港口现代化设施、钻石和铁矿开采技术、有限的铁路线（弗里敦至内陆）、福拉湾学院等教育机构。
keyInnovations.en: Freetown port modernization facilities, diamond and iron mining technology, a limited railway line (Freetown to the interior), and educational institutions such as Fourah Bay College.

strengths.zh: 弗里敦天然良港的战略价值、新发现的钻石资源潜力、悠久的西式教育传统（福拉湾学院）、克里奥尔精英的专业能力。
strengths.en: The strategic value of Freetown's natural harbor, the potential of newly discovered diamond resources, a long tradition of Western education (Fourah Bay College), and the professional capacity of the Krio elite.

weaknesses.zh: 殖民地与保护领之间的社会文化割裂、经济落后、基础设施严重不足、教育和医疗覆盖率极低（内陆）。
weaknesses.en: The socio-cultural divide between Colony and Protectorate, economic backwardness, severely inadequate infrastructure, and extremely low education and healthcare coverage (in the interior).

outlook.zh: 二战期间弗里敦将成为盟军大西洋护航的关键集结港口，战争经历将促进塞拉利昂的政治和社会变迁。
outlook.en: During WWII Freetown would become a key assembly port for Allied Atlantic convoys; the war experience would promote political and social change in Sierra Leone.

description.zh: 1939年的塞拉利昂是英国西非殖民地，弗里敦港具有重要的大西洋战略价值，克里奥尔精英文化与内陆部族社会构成独特的双重社会结构。
description.en: Sierra Leone in 1939 was a British West African colony whose Freetown port held important Atlantic strategic value, with the distinctive dual social structure of the Krio elite culture and interior tribal society.

governmentForm.zh: 英国直辖殖民地，总督拥有最高行政与立法权，保护国地区实行间接统治。
governmentForm.en: A British Crown Colony where the Governor held supreme executive and legislative authority, with indirect rule in the protectorate areas.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由英国殖民官员、巴瑟斯特克里奥尔商人精英、曼丁哥与沃洛夫等族群的酋长和农民组成。花生种植农民占人口绝大多数。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of British colonial officials, Creole merchant elites in Bathurst, chiefs and farmers from Mandinka, Wolof, and other ethnic groups. Groundnut-growing peasants formed the vast majority of the population.

rulingClass.zh: 英国总督及殖民官僚、巴瑟斯特商业精英、受承认的本地酋长。
rulingClass.en: The British Governor and colonial bureaucracy, Bathurst commercial elites, and recognized local chiefs.

succession.zh: 殖民最高权力由英国政府任命总督掌握；本地酋长职位依传统继承并须获殖民当局确认。
succession.en: Supreme colonial authority was vested in a Governor appointed by the British government; local chieftaincies followed traditional succession subject to colonial approval.

structure.zh: 冈比亚分为巴瑟斯特直辖殖民地和上游保护国两部分。总督主持行政委员会和立法委员会，保护国地区通过分区专员和酋长实行间接管理。
structure.en: The Gambia was divided into the Bathurst Crown Colony and the upriver Protectorate. The Governor presided over an Executive Council and a Legislative Council, while the Protectorate was managed indirectly through divisional commissioners and chiefs.

function.zh: 处理中央行政事务、预算编制、公文管理和对外联络。
function.en: Handled central administrative affairs, budget preparation, records management, and external liaison.

function.zh: 管理上游保护国地区的分区专员、酋长事务和税收征缴。
function.en: Managed divisional commissioners, chieftaincy affairs, and tax collection in the upriver Protectorate.

function.zh: 征收关税、花生出口税和执照费。
function.en: Collected customs duties, groundnut export taxes, and license fees.

localAdmin.zh: 保护国地区分设多个行政区，由分区专员管辖，依赖本地酋长维持秩序和征税。巴瑟斯特设有市政机构。
localAdmin.en: The Protectorate was divided into several divisions overseen by commissioners who relied on local chiefs for order and tax collection. Bathurst had a municipal administration.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合英国普通法、殖民条例和本地习惯法。巴瑟斯特适用英国法律，保护国地区习惯法与伊斯兰法并行。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined English common law, colonial ordinances, and local customary law. English law applied in Bathurst, while customary and Islamic law operated in the Protectorate.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以花生出口关税和进口关税为主，保护国征收人头税和牲畜税。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from groundnut export duties and import tariffs, with poll taxes and livestock taxes collected in the Protectorate.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教为主，少数基督教
religion.en: Predominantly Islam, with a Christian minority

culturalAchievements.zh: 巴瑟斯特是西非最早的英国殖民城镇之一，克里奥尔文化与曼丁哥口头传统并存。伊斯兰教育体系广泛覆盖。
culturalAchievements.en: Bathurst was one of the earliest British colonial towns in West Africa, with Creole culture coexisting alongside Mandinka oral traditions. Islamic educational networks were widespread.

languageFamily.zh: 英语属印欧语系日耳曼语族，曼丁哥语与沃洛夫语属尼日尔-刚果语系。
languageFamily.en: English belongs to the Indo-European Germanic family; Mandinka and Wolof belong to the Niger-Congo family.

philosophy.zh: 伊斯兰教法传统与西非传统信仰和社会习俗并行。
philosophy.en: Islamic legal tradition operated alongside West African traditional beliefs and social customs.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用英语拉丁字母；阿拉伯文用于宗教和伊斯兰教育；本地语言以阿贾米（阿拉伯字母书写当地语言）和口述为主。
writingSystem.en: English in Latin script was official; Arabic was used for religious and Islamic education; local languages were often written in Ajami (Arabic script) or transmitted orally.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济高度依赖花生单一种植和出口，严重受国际市场价格波动影响。工业几乎不存在。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was heavily dependent on groundnut monoculture and export, severely affected by international price fluctuations. Industry was virtually nonexistent.

mainIndustries.zh: 花生种植与出口、河运贸易、小规模渔业和手工业
mainIndustries.en: Groundnut cultivation and export, river trade, small-scale fishing, and handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 花生、花生油、蜂蜡、皮张、进口棉布、金属制品和食品
tradeGoods.en: Groundnuts, groundnut oil, beeswax, hides, imported cotton cloth, metalware, and foodstuffs

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在土地使用权、牲畜、花生存货和社区关系。巴瑟斯特商人拥有较多现金资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in land use rights, livestock, groundnut stocks, and community ties. Bathurst merchants held more cash assets.

economicSystem.zh: 以花生出口为主的殖民单一作物经济，由英国贸易公司主导。
economicSystem.en: A colonial monoculture economy dominated by groundnut exports and controlled by British trading firms.

treasuryDescription.zh: 殖民地库藏薄弱，财政严重依赖花生出口税收和英国补贴。
treasuryDescription.en: The colony's treasury was thin, heavily dependent on groundnut export revenue and British grants-in-aid.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持最低行政运作为目标，避免大规模公共投资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at maintaining minimum administrative operations, avoiding large-scale public investment.

name.zh: 冈比亚连队（英属西非前线部队）
name.en: Gambia Company (Royal West African Frontier Force)

description.zh: 隶属英属西非前线部队的小型殖民步兵连。
description.en: A small colonial infantry company attached to the Royal West African Frontier Force.

description.zh: 负责巴瑟斯特和主要城镇的治安与秩序维持。
description.en: Responsible for law enforcement and order in Bathurst and major towns.

commanderInChief.zh: 冈比亚总督兼英属西非前线部队当地指挥官
commanderInChief.en: Governor of the Gambia with local RWAFF command

technology.zh: 装备以步枪、轻机枪和基本通信设备为主，无重型武器或空军。
technology.en: Armed mainly with rifles, light machine guns, and basic communications equipment, with no heavy weapons or air force.

threats.zh: 直接军事威胁较低，主要安全关切为法属西非环绕下的战略脆弱性及大西洋潜艇战。
threats.en: Direct military threats were low; main security concerns were strategic vulnerability surrounded by French West Africa and the Atlantic submarine war.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

description.zh: 75-105mm榴弹炮，师级火力支援
description.en: 75-105mm howitzer, division-level fire support

populationDescription.zh: 人口以冈比亚河两岸的农业村落居民为主，巴瑟斯特是唯一城镇中心。族群以曼丁哥人、富拉人、沃洛夫人和朱拉人为主。
populationDescription.en: The population mainly consisted of agricultural villagers along both banks of the Gambia River, with Bathurst as the only urban center. Major ethnic groups included Mandinka, Fula, Wolof, and Jola.

socialClasses.zh: 英国殖民官员、克里奥尔商人、本地酋长、花生农民、河运商人和渔民。
socialClasses.en: British colonial officials, Creole merchants, local chiefs, groundnut farmers, river traders, and fishermen.

allies.zh: 英国帝国及其西非殖民地。
allies.en: The British Empire and its West African colonies.

enemies.zh: 无直接敌国，但与法属西非存在殖民竞争关系。
enemies.en: No direct enemies, but a colonial competitive relationship with French West Africa existed.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交由伦敦主导，地方重点维持与法属邻邦的合作关系和冈比亚河航运权。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed from London; local priorities centered on cooperative relations with neighboring French territories and navigation rights on the Gambia River.

era.zh: 殖民轻工业与河运时代
era.en: Colonial light industry and river transport era

keyInnovations.zh: 河运汽船、花生加工设备、有限公路、无线电通信。
keyInnovations.en: River steamships, groundnut processing equipment, limited roads, and radio communications.

strengths.zh: 冈比亚河提供天然航运通道，花生出口稳定，英国帝国体系提供安全保障。
strengths.en: The Gambia River provided a natural shipping corridor, groundnut exports were steady, and the British imperial system provided security.

weaknesses.zh: 经济高度单一，领土狭长被法属西非环绕，基础设施薄弱，人口稀少。
weaknesses.en: The economy was highly monocultural, the territory was a narrow strip surrounded by French West Africa, infrastructure was weak, and the population was small.

outlook.zh: 战争期间冈比亚将作为大西洋护航基地发挥一定战略作用，但经济发展前景有限。
outlook.en: During the war the Gambia would play a modest strategic role as an Atlantic convoy base, but economic development prospects remained limited.

description.zh: 1939年的冈比亚是英国在西非的小型殖民地，经济依赖花生出口，由英国总督直辖管理。
description.en: In 1939, the Gambia was a small British colony in West Africa, economically dependent on groundnut exports and governed by a British-appointed Governor.

name.zh: 尼日利亚殖民地与保护国

governmentForm.zh: 英属直辖殖民地与保护国联合体，北部实行间接统治（通过埃米尔与酋长），南部实行更直接的殖民管理。
governmentForm.en: A combined Crown Colony and Protectorate; the north practiced indirect rule through emirs and chiefs, while the south was under more direct colonial administration.

socialStructure.zh: 社会高度多元，北部以豪萨-富拉尼穆斯林酋长国体系为核心，南部约鲁巴城邦传统与伊博分散社会并存。拉各斯有西化精英阶层。
socialStructure.en: Society was highly diverse: the north centered on the Hausa-Fulani Muslim emirate system, the south combined Yoruba city-state traditions with Igbo decentralized communities. Lagos had a Westernized elite class.

rulingClass.zh: 英国总督与殖民官僚、北部埃米尔与酋长、南部约鲁巴王公和拉各斯受过西方教育的精英。
rulingClass.en: The British Governor and colonial bureaucracy, northern emirs and chiefs, southern Yoruba rulers, and Western-educated elites in Lagos.

succession.zh: 殖民最高权力由英国任命总督掌握；北部埃米尔世袭继承须获英方批准；南部酋长权力因地区传统而异。
succession.en: Supreme colonial authority lay with the British-appointed Governor; northern emirs followed hereditary succession subject to British approval; southern chieftaincy varied by regional tradition.

structure.zh: 尼日利亚分为拉各斯殖民地、南部省份和北部省份三大行政单元。总督主持中央行政委员会和立法委员会。北部省份由驻拉各斯的副总督统辖，实行以本地埃米尔制度为基础的间接统治。
structure.en: Nigeria was divided into three administrative units: Lagos Colony, Southern Provinces, and Northern Provinces. The Governor presided over a central Executive and Legislative Council. The Northern Provinces were overseen by a Lieutenant-Governor based in Kaduna, operating indirect rule through the indigenous emirate system.

function.zh: 统筹全国行政事务、法规拟定、公文管理和帝国联络。
function.en: Coordinated national administrative affairs, regulation drafting, records management, and imperial liaison.

name.en: Department of Internal Affairs and Native Administration

function.zh: 监管本地行政当局、酋长事务、间接统治机制和地区政务。
function.en: Supervised native authorities, chieftaincy affairs, indirect rule mechanisms, and regional administration.

function.zh: 管理进出口关税、内陆税收和财政收支。
function.en: Managed import-export duties, inland revenue, and fiscal accounts.

function.zh: 负责铁路、公路、港口和公共建筑的建设与维护。
function.en: Responsible for construction and maintenance of railways, roads, harbors, and public buildings.

localAdmin.zh: 北部通过埃米尔、区长和村长实行多层间接统治；南部设有省长、分区官员和本地法院，约鲁巴地区酋长权力较大，伊博地区则由殖民当局指派的授权酋长管辖。
localAdmin.en: The north operated multi-layered indirect rule through emirs, district heads, and village chiefs; the south had provincial residents, district officers, and native courts, with Yoruba chiefs holding substantial power and Igbo areas governed through warrant chiefs appointed by colonial authorities.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合英国普通法、殖民法令、伊斯兰法（北部）和本地习惯法。拉各斯以英国法为主，北部主要适用伊斯兰法。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined English common law, colonial ordinances, Islamic law (in the north), and local customary law. Lagos primarily used English law, while the north mainly applied Islamic law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以关税和出口税为大宗，北部征收本地税（哈拉吉税），南部设有直接税和本地行政税。锡矿特许权使用费亦为重要收入。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue was dominated by customs and export duties; the north collected native taxes (haraji), the south had direct taxes and native administration levies. Tin mining royalties were also significant.

religion.zh: 北部以伊斯兰教为主，南部基督教和传统宗教并存
religion.en: Islam predominated in the north; Christianity and traditional religions coexisted in the south

culturalAchievements.zh: 贝宁青铜艺术传统、伊费雕塑、豪萨建筑、约鲁巴口头文学和诗歌、拉各斯的现代新闻出版业。
culturalAchievements.en: Benin bronze art tradition, Ife sculptures, Hausa architecture, Yoruba oral literature and poetry, and the modern press in Lagos.

languageFamily.zh: 英语属印欧语系日耳曼语族；豪萨语属亚非语系乍得语族；约鲁巴语和伊博语属尼日尔-刚果语系。
languageFamily.en: English belongs to the Indo-European Germanic family; Hausa to the Afro-Asiatic Chadic family; Yoruba and Igbo to the Niger-Congo family.

philosophy.zh: 伊斯兰法学传统（北部）、约鲁巴宇宙观与伊法占卜体系、基督教现代化思想（拉各斯精英圈）。
philosophy.en: Islamic jurisprudence (north), Yoruba cosmology and Ifa divination, and Christian modernizing thought (Lagos elite circles).

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用英语拉丁字母；北部广泛使用阿贾米（阿拉伯字母书写豪萨语）；约鲁巴语和伊博语在传教士影响下使用拉丁字母书写。
writingSystem.en: English in Latin script was official; Ajami (Arabic-script Hausa) was widespread in the north; Yoruba and Igbo used Latin script under missionary influence.

gdpDescription.zh: 英属非洲最大经济体之一，以农产品出口（可可、花生、棕榈油）和锡矿开采为支柱。工业化程度低，经济严重依赖原材料出口。
gdpDescription.en: One of the largest economies in British Africa, supported by agricultural exports (cocoa, groundnuts, palm oil) and tin mining. Industrialization was low, with the economy heavily dependent on raw material exports.

mainIndustries.zh: 可可种植、花生种植、棕榈油生产、锡矿开采、煤矿、铁路运输
mainIndustries.en: Cocoa cultivation, groundnut farming, palm oil production, tin mining, coal mining, and railway transport

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、花生、棕榈油与棕榈仁、锡矿石、煤炭、皮张、进口纺织品和制造品
tradeGoods.en: Cocoa, groundnuts, palm oil and palm kernels, tin ore, coal, hides, and imported textiles and manufactures

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富因地区差异巨大：北部以牲畜和土地为主，可可产区以种植园和存货为主，拉各斯精英拥有房产和商业资本。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied enormously by region: livestock and land in the north, plantations and stocks in cocoa areas, and property and commercial capital among Lagos elites.

economicSystem.zh: 以出口导向型殖民经济为核心，英国贸易公司控制主要贸易渠道，本地市场经济与自给农业并存。
economicSystem.en: An export-oriented colonial economy with British trading firms controlling major trade channels, alongside local market economies and subsistence agriculture.

treasuryDescription.zh: 尼日利亚是少数财政自给的英属非洲殖民地之一，财政状况相对健康，依靠出口税和矿业收入维持盈余。
treasuryDescription.en: Nigeria was one of the few financially self-sustaining British African colonies, with relatively healthy finances maintained through export duties and mining revenue.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以出口税收为基础，优先保障铁路与港口基建投入，同时维持间接统治体制下的本地行政开支。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy relied on export tax revenue, prioritizing railway and port infrastructure investment while maintaining local administrative expenditure under the indirect rule system.

name.zh: 尼日利亚团（英属西非前线部队）
name.en: Nigeria Regiment (Royal West African Frontier Force)

description.zh: 英属西非前线部队主力，分驻北部和南部多个驻地。
description.en: The main force of the Royal West African Frontier Force, garrisoned at multiple posts in the north and south.

description.zh: 负责全国城镇治安、刑事侦查和秩序维持。
description.en: Responsible for law enforcement, criminal investigation, and order maintenance in towns nationwide.

description.zh: 由本地行政当局管理的辅助警力，协助间接统治地区的治安。
description.en: Auxiliary police managed by native authorities, assisting with security in indirectly ruled areas.

commanderInChief.zh: 尼日利亚总督兼英属西非前线部队尼日利亚军区司令
commanderInChief.en: Governor of Nigeria and Commander of the Nigeria District, RWAFF

technology.zh: 装备包括步枪、轻机枪、迫击炮、卡车运输和无线电通信。战争期间尼日利亚部队将被扩编并部署至东非和缅甸战场。
technology.en: Equipment included rifles, light machine guns, mortars, truck transport, and radio communications. During the war, Nigerian forces would be expanded and deployed to East Africa and Burma.

threats.zh: 主要威胁包括法属殖民地在维希法国统治下的潜在敌意、大西洋潜艇战对海运的威胁，以及内部族群矛盾。
threats.en: Major threats included potential hostility from French colonies under Vichy rule, Atlantic submarine warfare threatening shipping, and internal ethnic tensions.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

description.zh: T-34/谢尔曼/四号坦克，装甲战核心
description.en: T-34/Sherman/Panzer IV medium tank, core of armored warfare

populationDescription.zh: 非洲人口最多的殖民地，族群高度多样化：北部以豪萨-富拉尼人为主，西南部为约鲁巴人，东南部为伊博人，此外有努佩、蒂夫、伊贾等数百个少数族群。
populationDescription.en: The most populous colony in Africa, with extreme ethnic diversity: Hausa-Fulani in the north, Yoruba in the southwest, Igbo in the southeast, plus hundreds of minority groups including Nupe, Tiv, and Ijaw.

description.zh: 非洲最大经济中心
description.en: Africa's largest economic center

socialClasses.zh: 英国殖民官员、拉各斯西化精英与知识分子、北部埃米尔和贵族、约鲁巴王公和酋长、大商人、小商贩、自耕农和矿工。
socialClasses.en: British colonial officials, Lagos Westernized elites and intellectuals, northern emirs and aristocracy, Yoruba kings and chiefs, major merchants, petty traders, peasant farmers, and miners.

allies.zh: 英国帝国、英属西非其他殖民地。
allies.en: The British Empire and other British West African colonies.

enemies.zh: 无直接敌国，但法属殖民地在维希政权下可能构成潜在对手。
enemies.en: No direct enemies, but French colonies under the Vichy regime could become potential adversaries.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交由伦敦殖民部主导，地方关注维持与法属西非的边境稳定和区域贸易关系。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed by the Colonial Office in London; local concerns focused on maintaining stable borders with French West Africa and regional trade relations.

era.zh: 殖民铁路与矿业开发时代
era.en: Colonial railway and mining development era

keyInnovations.zh: 铁路网络（拉各斯至卡诺线、东线）、锡矿现代采掘技术、拉各斯港口设施、无线电台、有限公路系统。
keyInnovations.en: Railway network (Lagos-Kano line, Eastern line), modern tin mining techniques, Lagos port facilities, radio stations, and a limited road system.

strengths.zh: 庞大人口基数、丰富自然资源（锡、煤、农产品）、铁路基础设施较完善、财政自给。
strengths.en: Large population base, abundant natural resources (tin, coal, agricultural products), relatively developed railway infrastructure, and financial self-sufficiency.

weaknesses.zh: 工业化程度极低、族群与宗教分裂严重、南北发展差距巨大、公共教育和卫生覆盖率低。
weaknesses.en: Extremely low industrialization, severe ethnic and religious divisions, vast north-south development gap, and low public education and health coverage.

outlook.zh: 战争将推动尼日利亚军事动员和经济重要性提升，战后民族主义运动将迅速壮大。
outlook.en: The war would boost Nigeria's military mobilization and economic importance, while post-war nationalist movements would rapidly strengthen.

description.zh: 1939年的尼日利亚是英属非洲最大的殖民地，人口约两千万，经济以农产品和矿产出口为支柱，北部实行间接统治，南部殖民管理较为直接。
description.en: In 1939, Nigeria was the largest British colony in Africa with a population of about 20 million, an economy based on agricultural and mineral exports, indirect rule in the north, and more direct colonial administration in the south.

governmentForm.zh: 国际联盟法国托管地，由法国驻地专员实行直接殖民管理，名义上受国联监督。
governmentForm.en: A League of Nations mandate under France, administered directly by a French Commissioner, nominally supervised by the League.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以埃维族和多个北部族群的农业社区为基础，法国殖民官员和少数商人构成上层。强迫劳动制度仍部分存在。
socialStructure.en: Society was based on agricultural communities of the Ewe and various northern ethnic groups, with French colonial officials and a small merchant class forming the upper stratum. Forced labor practices still partially existed.

rulingClass.zh: 法国殖民专员与行政官员、受认可的本地酋长、少数欧洲和黎凡特商人。
rulingClass.en: The French Commissioner and administrative officials, recognized local chiefs, and a small number of European and Levantine merchants.

succession.zh: 殖民最高权力由法国政府任命专员掌握；本地酋长职位按传统继承并须获法国当局确认。
succession.en: Supreme authority was held by a Commissioner appointed by the French government; local chieftaincies followed traditional succession confirmed by French authorities.

structure.zh: 法属多哥由驻洛美的法国专员直接管理，设有行政秘书处和地方行政区。与法属西非联邦行政上独立，但实际上联系紧密。
structure.en: French Togoland was directly administered by a French Commissioner in Lomé, with an administrative secretariat and local districts. Administratively separate from the French West African federation but closely linked in practice.

function.zh: 处理中央行政、预算和对巴黎及国联的报告事务。
function.en: Handled central administration, budgeting, and reporting to Paris and the League of Nations.

function.zh: 管理各行政区的治安、税收和本地事务。
function.en: Managed security, tax collection, and local affairs across administrative districts.

function.zh: 征收关税、出口税和直接税。
function.en: Collected customs duties, export taxes, and direct taxes.

localAdmin.zh: 地方分设行政区，由法国区长管辖，本地酋长在税收征缴和劳动征发中起辅助作用。
localAdmin.en: The territory was divided into administrative districts overseen by French commandants, with local chiefs serving auxiliary roles in tax collection and labor recruitment.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以法国殖民法为基础，辅以本地习惯法。本地人受indigenat制度管辖，法国公民适用法国法律。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based on French colonial law supplemented by local customary law. Natives were subject to the indigénat system, while French citizens followed French law.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以人头税、关税和出口税为主。强迫劳动制度作为一种隐性征税形式仍部分存在。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from head taxes, customs duties, and export taxes. Forced labor served as a form of implicit taxation and still partially existed.

religion.zh: 传统宗教为主，基督教（天主教）和伊斯兰教并存
religion.en: Traditional religions predominated, with Christianity (Catholicism) and Islam also present

culturalAchievements.zh: 埃维族鼓乐和织布传统、天主教传教学校教育体系、洛美沿海贸易文化。
culturalAchievements.en: Ewe drumming and weaving traditions, Catholic mission school education system, and Lomé coastal trading culture.

languageFamily.zh: 法语属印欧语系罗曼语族；埃维语属尼日尔-刚果语系跨圭语族；卡比耶语等属圭尔语支。
languageFamily.en: French belongs to the Indo-European Romance family; Ewe to the Niger-Congo Gbe family; Kabiyé and others to the Gur branch.

philosophy.zh: 传统信仰与天主教传教士教育并行，伏都教信仰体系在南部影响广泛。
philosophy.en: Traditional beliefs operated alongside Catholic missionary education, with Vodun belief systems widespread in the south.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用法语拉丁字母；传教士以拉丁字母书写埃维语；大部分族群以口述传统为主。
writingSystem.en: French in Latin script was official; missionaries wrote Ewe in Latin script; most ethnic groups relied primarily on oral traditions.

gdpDescription.zh: 小型殖民经济，以可可、咖啡、棕榈产品出口为主。洛美港是主要贸易口岸。
gdpDescription.en: A small colonial economy based mainly on cocoa, coffee, and palm product exports. Lomé port was the main trade hub.

mainIndustries.zh: 可可种植、咖啡种植、棕榈油生产、小规模磷酸盐开采、渔业
mainIndustries.en: Cocoa cultivation, coffee growing, palm oil production, small-scale phosphate mining, and fishing

tradeGoods.zh: 可可、咖啡、棕榈油与棕榈仁、棉花、进口制造品和纺织品
tradeGoods.en: Cocoa, coffee, palm oil and palm kernels, cotton, and imported manufactures and textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富主要体现在农业种植园、土地使用权和牲畜。沿海商人有较多商业资本。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly held in agricultural plantations, land use rights, and livestock. Coastal merchants held more commercial capital.

economicSystem.zh: 以出口型殖民种植经济为核心，法国贸易公司主导商业流通。
economicSystem.en: An export-oriented colonial plantation economy dominated by French trading firms.

treasuryDescription.zh: 托管地财政规模小，勉强维持平衡，部分依赖法国补贴。
treasuryDescription.en: The mandate's finances were small, barely balanced, and partly dependent on French subsidies.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 以维持基本行政运作和出口经济为目标，公共投资极为有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed at maintaining basic administration and the export economy, with very limited public investment.

description.zh: 法属殖民步兵部队，负责领土防御和内部治安。
description.en: French colonial infantry troops responsible for territorial defense and internal security.

description.zh: 负责洛美及各区治安和执法。
description.en: Responsible for law enforcement and security in Lomé and districts.

commanderInChief.zh: 法属多哥驻地专员兼军事指挥官
commanderInChief.en: Commissioner of French Togoland with military command

technology.zh: 装备以步枪和轻武器为主，无重型装备或空军。
technology.en: Armed mainly with rifles and light weapons, with no heavy equipment or air force.

threats.zh: 直接军事威胁较低，地处英属黄金海岸和法属达荷美之间。
threats.en: Direct military threats were low, situated between the British Gold Coast and French Dahomey.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

description.zh: 破片手榴弹，步兵必备
description.en: Fragmentation hand grenade, infantry essential

populationDescription.zh: 人口以南部埃维族和北部卡比耶等族群为主，多数为农业人口。洛美是唯一较大城镇。
populationDescription.en: The population consisted mainly of Ewe in the south and Kabiyé and other groups in the north, mostly agricultural. Lomé was the only sizeable town.

socialClasses.zh: 法国殖民官员、沿海商人、本地酋长、种植园农民和自给农民。
socialClasses.en: French colonial officials, coastal merchants, local chiefs, plantation farmers, and subsistence farmers.

allies.zh: 法国及法属西非殖民地。
allies.en: France and French West African colonies.

enemies.zh: 无直接敌国。
enemies.en: No direct enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交由巴黎主导，地方维持与英属黄金海岸的边境合作。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed from Paris; locally maintained cooperative border relations with the British Gold Coast.

era.zh: 殖民种植经济时代

keyInnovations.zh: 洛美港口码头、有限铁路支线、可可加工设施和传教学校。
keyInnovations.en: Lomé port wharf, limited railway spur lines, cocoa processing facilities, and mission schools.

strengths.zh: 沿海港口便利、可可和咖啡种植条件良好、族群关系相对稳定。
strengths.en: Convenient coastal port, good cocoa and coffee growing conditions, and relatively stable ethnic relations.

weaknesses.zh: 经济规模极小、领土面积有限、基础设施不足、教育水平低。
weaknesses.en: Very small economic scale, limited territory, insufficient infrastructure, and low educational attainment.

outlook.zh: 战争爆发后多哥将迅速倒向自由法国，其战略重要性有限。
outlook.en: After the war's outbreak, Togo would quickly align with Free France, though its strategic importance remained limited.

description.zh: 1939年的法属多哥是国联法国托管地，经济以可可和咖啡出口为主，由法国专员直接管理的小型殖民领地。
description.en: In 1939, French Togoland was a League of Nations mandate under France, a small colonial territory directly administered by a French Commissioner with an economy based on cocoa and coffee exports.

governmentForm.zh: 法属西非联邦下的殖民地，由法国总督直接管理，本地酋长作为辅助行政力量。
governmentForm.en: A colony within the French West African federation, directly administered by a French Governor with local chiefs serving as auxiliary administrative agents.

socialStructure.zh: 社会以丰族为主导，原达荷美王国贵族后裔仍有社会影响力，沿海有约鲁巴和贡族社区。法国殖民官员和黎凡特商人构成上层。
socialStructure.en: Society was dominated by the Fon people, with descendants of the former Dahomey Kingdom nobility retaining social influence. Yoruba and Gun communities existed along the coast. French colonial officials and Levantine merchants formed the upper stratum.

rulingClass.zh: 法国总督与殖民官僚、受承认的传统酋长、少数欧洲和黎凡特商人。
rulingClass.en: The French Governor and colonial bureaucracy, recognized traditional chiefs, and a small number of European and Levantine merchants.

succession.zh: 殖民最高权力由法国政府任命总督掌握；本地酋长职位按传统继承并须获殖民当局认可。
succession.en: Supreme authority was held by a Governor appointed by the French government; local chieftaincies followed traditional succession confirmed by colonial authorities.

structure.zh: 达荷美作为法属西非联邦成员，由驻波多诺伏的总督管理，下设若干行政区。实际行政中心在科托努。殖民政府对国联的汇报义务较少。
structure.en: As a member of the French West African federation, Dahomey was governed by a Governor based in Porto-Novo, with several administrative districts. The de facto administrative center was Cotonou. The colonial government had limited League of Nations reporting obligations.

function.zh: 管理中央行政事务、财政预算和对法属西非联邦总部的联络。
function.en: Managed central administrative affairs, fiscal budgets, and liaison with the French West Africa federation headquarters.

name.zh: 地方事务与酋长管理局

function.zh: 监管各区行政、酋长事务和税收征缴。
function.en: Oversaw district administration, chieftaincy affairs, and tax collection.

function.zh: 征收关税、出口税和人头税。
function.en: Collected customs duties, export taxes, and head taxes.

function.zh: 负责道路、港口和公共建筑建设。
function.en: Responsible for roads, port facilities, and public building construction.

localAdmin.zh: 地方分设行政区，由法国区长管辖，本地酋长在税收征缴和劳动力调配中起辅助作用。indigenat制度赋予行政官员对本地人的即时惩罚权。
localAdmin.en: The territory was divided into administrative districts overseen by French commandants, with local chiefs serving auxiliary roles in tax collection and labor recruitment. The indigénat system gave officials summary punitive power over natives.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以法国殖民法为主，辅以本地习惯法。本地人受indigenat制度管辖。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was primarily French colonial law supplemented by local customary law. Natives were subject to the indigénat system.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以人头税、关税和棕榈产品出口税为主。强迫劳动制度作为隐性赋税仍部分存在。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came mainly from head taxes, customs duties, and palm product export taxes. Forced labor as implicit taxation still partially existed.

religion.zh: 传统宗教（伏都教）为主，天主教和伊斯兰教并存
religion.en: Traditional religions (Vodun) predominated, with Catholicism and Islam also present

culturalAchievements.zh: 达荷美王国浮雕艺术传统、伏都教仪式文化、丰族口头历史传统、天主教传教学校培养了西非最早一批受法式教育的精英。
culturalAchievements.en: Dahomey Kingdom relief art tradition, Vodun ceremonial culture, Fon oral history traditions, and Catholic mission schools produced some of the earliest French-educated elites in West Africa.

languageFamily.zh: 法语属印欧语系罗曼语族；丰语和贡语属尼日尔-刚果语系跨圭语族。
languageFamily.en: French belongs to the Indo-European Romance family; Fon and Gun belong to the Niger-Congo Gbe family.

philosophy.zh: 伏都教宇宙观与天主教传教士教育并行，达荷美知识分子在法语教育中发展早期非洲民族主义思想。
philosophy.en: Vodun cosmology operated alongside Catholic missionary education; Dahomean intellectuals developed early African nationalist thought through French-language education.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用法语拉丁字母；传教士以拉丁字母书写丰语和约鲁巴语；多数族群以口述为主。
writingSystem.en: French in Latin script was official; missionaries wrote Fon and Yoruba in Latin script; most ethnic groups relied primarily on oral tradition.

gdpDescription.zh: 殖民经济以棕榈油和棕榈仁出口为支柱，科托努港是主要贸易口岸。达荷美是法属西非受教育程度较高的殖民地之一。
gdpDescription.en: The colonial economy was based on palm oil and palm kernel exports, with Cotonou port as the main trade hub. Dahomey was one of the more educated colonies in French West Africa.

mainIndustries.zh: 棕榈油生产、棕榈仁加工、自给农业、小规模渔业和贸易
mainIndustries.en: Palm oil production, palm kernel processing, subsistence agriculture, small-scale fishing, and trade

tradeGoods.zh: 棕榈油、棕榈仁、玉米、棉花、进口纺织品和制造品
tradeGoods.en: Palm oil, palm kernels, maize, cotton, and imported textiles and manufactures

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富以棕榈种植园、土地使用权和牲畜为主。沿海商人和受过教育的精英拥有更多现代资产。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was held mainly in palm plantations, land rights, and livestock. Coastal merchants and educated elites held more modern assets.

economicSystem.zh: 以棕榈产品出口为核心的殖民种植经济，法国贸易公司控制主要流通渠道。
economicSystem.en: A colonial plantation economy centered on palm product exports, with French trading firms controlling major distribution channels.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政规模较小但相对稳定，棕榈出口税收是主要来源。
treasuryDescription.en: Finances were small but relatively stable, with palm export taxes as the main source.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以维持殖民行政和出口经济为优先，教育投入在法属西非中偏高。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized colonial administration and the export economy, with education spending relatively high for French West Africa.

description.zh: 法属殖民步兵部队，为法军重要兵源地之一。
description.en: French colonial infantry troops; Dahomey was an important recruitment source for the French army.

description.zh: 负责城镇治安、执法和内部秩序。
description.en: Responsible for urban security, law enforcement, and internal order.

commanderInChief.zh: 达荷美总督兼法属西非联邦军事当局
commanderInChief.en: Governor of Dahomey under the French West Africa federation military authority

technology.zh: 装备以步枪和轻武器为主，依赖法属西非联邦军事指挥体系。
technology.en: Armed mainly with rifles and light weapons, relying on the French West Africa federation military command system.

threats.zh: 直接军事威胁较低，主要安全关切为内部社会紧张和法国本土战争期间的政治动荡传导。
threats.en: Direct military threats were low; main concerns were internal social tensions and the transmission of political upheaval from wartime metropolitan France.

description.zh: 基础训练，民兵水平
description.en: Basic training, militia level

description.zh: 士气极低，厌战情绪蔓延
description.en: Very low morale, widespread war-weariness

description.zh: 汤普森/MP40/PPSh-41冲锋枪，近战火力猛
description.en: Thompson/MP40/PPSh-41 SMGs with fierce close-quarters firepower

description.zh: M1加兰德/SVT-40等半自动步枪
description.en: M1 Garand/SVT-40 semi-automatic rifles

populationDescription.zh: 人口以南部丰族和约鲁巴族为主，北部有巴里巴、索姆巴等族群。多数为农业人口，教育水平在法属西非中相对较高。
populationDescription.en: The population was mainly Fon and Yoruba in the south, with Bariba, Somba, and other groups in the north. Most people were agricultural; education levels were relatively high for French West Africa.

socialClasses.zh: 法国殖民官员、受法式教育的本地精英、传统酋长与贵族后裔、商人、农民和渔民。
socialClasses.en: French colonial officials, French-educated local elites, traditional chiefs and noble descendants, merchants, farmers, and fishermen.

allies.zh: 法国及法属西非联邦成员。
allies.en: France and members of the French West African federation.

enemies.zh: 无直接敌国。
enemies.en: No direct enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交由巴黎和达喀尔（法属西非联邦总部）主导。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed from Paris and Dakar (French West Africa federation headquarters).

era.zh: 殖民港口贸易时代

keyInnovations.zh: 科托努港口设施、有限铁路线、棕榈油加工工厂和传教学校体系。
keyInnovations.en: Cotonou port facilities, limited railway lines, palm oil processing plants, and the mission school system.

strengths.zh: 棕榈油产量稳定、沿海港口便利、教育水平在法属西非中领先。
strengths.en: Steady palm oil production, convenient coastal ports, and leading education levels in French West Africa.

weaknesses.zh: 经济规模小且依赖单一出口商品、工业化程度极低、基础设施不足。
weaknesses.en: Small economic scale dependent on a single export commodity, extremely low industrialization, and insufficient infrastructure.

outlook.zh: 达荷美受过教育的精英将在战后成为法属西非民族独立运动的重要力量。
outlook.en: Dahomey's educated elite would become an important force in post-war national independence movements in French West Africa.

description.zh: 1939年的法属达荷美是法属西非联邦成员，经济以棕榈产品出口为主，拥有法属西非较高的教育水平。
description.en: In 1939, French Dahomey was a member of the French West African federation, with an economy based on palm product exports and relatively high education levels for French West Africa.

dynasty.zh: 侯赛因王朝（法国保护统治）

governmentForm.zh: 名义上由突尼斯贝伊统治的法国保护国，实际权力掌握在法国驻扎总理大臣手中。
governmentForm.en: Nominally ruled by the Bey of Tunis as a French protectorate, with real power held by the French Resident-General.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由法国殖民定居者（约15万）、突尼斯阿拉伯-柏柏尔穆斯林多数民众、意大利侨民社区和犹太社区构成。法国定居者和犹太人享有较高经济地位。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of French settlers (about 150,000), a Tunisian Arab-Berber Muslim majority, an Italian expatriate community, and a Jewish community. French settlers and Jews held higher economic status.

rulingClass.zh: 法国驻扎总理大臣与殖民行政机构、贝伊宫廷、法国定居者精英和突尼斯大地主。
rulingClass.en: The French Resident-General and colonial administration, the Beylic court, French settler elites, and Tunisian large landowners.

succession.zh: 贝伊王位在侯赛因家族内继承，但须获法国当局批准。实际行政权力由法国任命的驻扎总理大臣掌握。
succession.en: The Beylic throne was inherited within the Husainid family but required French approval. Actual administrative power was held by the French-appointed Resident-General.

structure.zh: 突尼斯名义上保留贝伊政府架构，但法国驻扎总理大臣控制所有重要行政决策。法国官员管理财政、司法和安全部门，贝伊的法令须经驻扎总理大臣副署。
structure.en: Tunisia nominally retained the Beylic governmental structure, but the French Resident-General controlled all significant administrative decisions. French officials managed finance, justice, and security, and the Bey's decrees required the Resident-General's countersignature.

function.zh: 统筹全部殖民行政事务，监督贝伊政府，管理法国定居者事务。
function.en: Coordinated all colonial administrative affairs, supervised the Beylic government, and managed French settler affairs.

function.zh: 管理税收、海关、预算和公共财务。
function.en: Managed taxation, customs, budgets, and public finances.

name.en: Directorate of Interior and Local Administration

function.zh: 监管地方行政、市政事务和本地酋长管理。
function.en: Supervised local administration, municipal affairs, and native authority management.

function.zh: 负责铁路、公路、港口和灌溉设施建设。
function.en: Responsible for railways, roads, ports, and irrigation infrastructure.

localAdmin.zh: 地方分设多个省份和区，由法国任命的民政官员管理，市镇有地方议会但权力有限。突尼斯人可参与咨询性大议会。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was divided into provinces and districts managed by French-appointed civil officials, with municipal councils of limited power. Tunisians could participate in a consultative Grand Council.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合法国民法、伊斯兰沙里亚法（适用于穆斯林个人事务）和拉比法（适用于犹太社区）。法国法院与本地法院并存。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined French civil law, Islamic sharia (for Muslim personal affairs), and rabbinical law (for the Jewish community). French and native courts coexisted.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收包括关税、土地税、什一税（经改革）、营业税和专营收入。法国定居者享有税收优惠。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue included customs duties, land taxes, reformed tithe taxes, business taxes, and monopoly revenues. French settlers enjoyed tax advantages.

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教为主，天主教（法国和意大利定居者）和犹太教并存
religion.en: Islam predominated, with Catholicism (French and Italian settlers) and Judaism also present

culturalAchievements.zh: 突尼斯古城和宰图纳清真寺大学是伊斯兰学术重镇；法语文学和新闻出版业发展；新宪政党推动的现代民族主义运动。
culturalAchievements.en: The Medina of Tunis and Zitouna Mosque University were centers of Islamic scholarship; French-language literature and press developed; the Neo-Destour party drove the modern nationalist movement.

languageFamily.zh: 法语属印欧语系罗曼语族；阿拉伯语属亚非语系闪语族；柏柏尔语属亚非语系柏柏尔语族。
languageFamily.en: French belongs to the Indo-European Romance family; Arabic to the Afro-Asiatic Semitic family; Berber to the Afro-Asiatic Berber family.

philosophy.zh: 马利基派伊斯兰法学与现代阿拉伯民族主义思潮并行，新宪政党融合了法国共和主义和伊斯兰改革主义。
philosophy.en: Maliki Islamic jurisprudence coexisted with modern Arab nationalist thought; the Neo-Destour party blended French republicanism with Islamic reformism.

writingSystem.zh: 官方使用法语拉丁字母和阿拉伯文；突尼斯阿拉伯方言以阿拉伯字母书写。
writingSystem.en: French in Latin script and Arabic were both officially used; Tunisian Arabic was written in Arabic script.

gdpDescription.zh: 北非较发达的殖民经济体，以农业（橄榄油、谷物、葡萄酒）、磷酸盐开采和对法贸易为支柱。法国定居者控制大部分商业和优质农地。
gdpDescription.en: A relatively developed North African colonial economy, based on agriculture (olive oil, grain, wine), phosphate mining, and trade with France. French settlers controlled most commerce and prime agricultural land.

mainIndustries.zh: 橄榄油生产、谷物种植、葡萄酒酿造、磷酸盐开采、渔业、纺织手工业
mainIndustries.en: Olive oil production, grain cultivation, wine making, phosphate mining, fishing, and textile handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 磷酸盐、橄榄油、葡萄酒、谷物、铁矿石、进口制造品和机械
tradeGoods.en: Phosphates, olive oil, wine, grain, iron ore, and imported manufactures and machinery

householdWealth.zh: 财富分配极度不平等：法国定居者拥有大量优质农地和商业资产，城市突尼斯人以手工业和小贸易为主，农村多数为佃农或小自耕农。
householdWealth.en: Wealth distribution was highly unequal: French settlers owned large tracts of prime farmland and commercial assets, urban Tunisians relied on handicrafts and small trade, and most rural people were sharecroppers or smallholders.

economicSystem.zh: 以对法出口为导向的殖民经济，法国定居者和大型企业控制关键经济部门。
economicSystem.en: A colonial economy oriented toward exports to France, with French settlers and major enterprises controlling key economic sectors.

treasuryDescription.zh: 财政状况相对健康，磷酸盐收入和农业出口税提供稳定收入来源。
treasuryDescription.en: Finances were relatively healthy, with phosphate revenues and agricultural export taxes providing stable income.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政以支持法国定居者经济和基础设施建设为优先，对穆斯林人口的社会支出偏低。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized supporting the French settler economy and infrastructure, with relatively low social spending on the Muslim population.

name.zh: 突尼斯殖民步兵（蒂拉耶尔）

description.zh: 由突尼斯穆斯林士兵和法国军官组成的殖民步兵团。
description.en: Colonial infantry regiments composed of Tunisian Muslim soldiers and French officers.

description.zh: 驻扎在突尼斯的法国本土部队，承担战略防御任务。
description.en: French metropolitan troops garrisoned in Tunisia for strategic defense.

description.zh: 负责内部治安和秩序维持。
description.en: Responsible for internal security and order maintenance.

commanderInChief.zh: 法国驻突尼斯军事指挥官
commanderInChief.en: French Military Commander in Tunisia

technology.zh: 装备包括步枪、机枪、火炮、装甲车和有限空军力量。战争爆发后突尼斯将成为北非战役的关键战场。
technology.en: Equipment included rifles, machine guns, artillery, armored vehicles, and limited air assets. After war broke out, Tunisia would become a key battleground in the North African campaign.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自意大利在利比亚的军事力量和地中海控制权争夺，以及内部民族主义运动带来的政治不稳定。
threats.en: Main threats came from Italian military forces in Libya and the contest for Mediterranean control, as well as political instability from internal nationalist movements.

description.zh: 常规训练体系，兵种有基本配合
description.en: Regular training regimen with basic unit coordination

description.zh: 士气低迷，纪律涣散
description.en: Low morale, weakening discipline

description.zh: MG42/勃朗宁M2重机枪，火力压制核心
description.en: MG42/Browning M2 HMG, core fire suppression weapon

description.zh: T-34/谢尔曼/四号坦克，装甲战核心
description.en: T-34/Sherman/Panzer IV medium tank, core of armored warfare

description.zh: 破片手榴弹，步兵必备
description.en: Fragmentation hand grenade, infantry essential

populationDescription.zh: 人口以阿拉伯-柏柏尔穆斯林为主体（约230万），法国定居者约15万，意大利侨民约10万，犹太社区约6万。
populationDescription.en: The population was predominantly Arab-Berber Muslim (about 2.3 million), with approximately 150,000 French settlers, 100,000 Italian expatriates, and 60,000 in the Jewish community.

socialClasses.zh: 法国殖民官员与定居者精英、突尼斯大地主和商人、城市手工业者和知识分子、农村佃农和小农、意大利工人和犹太商人。
socialClasses.en: French colonial officials and settler elites, Tunisian large landowners and merchants, urban artisans and intellectuals, rural sharecroppers and smallholders, Italian workers, and Jewish merchants.

allies.zh: 法国及法国殖民帝国。
allies.en: France and the French colonial empire.

enemies.zh: 意大利是主要潜在敌国，法西斯意大利对突尼斯有领土野心。
enemies.en: Italy was the main potential enemy, with Fascist Italy harboring territorial ambitions over Tunisia.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交由巴黎主导。突尼斯地处地中海中心，是法国北非防御体系的重要环节。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was directed from Paris. Tunisia's central Mediterranean location made it a vital link in France's North African defense system.
